87 double node_ub = nodes[node].ub[r];
88
89 // Global bounds (if needed for computing splits).
90 double global_lb = R_min[r];
91 double global_ub = R_max[r];
92 double full_range = global_ub - global_lb;
93 double node_range = node_ub - node_lb;
94
95 // Determine the number of splits (i.e. intervals) for resource r.
96 int splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round((node_range / full_range) * intervals[r].interval)));
97 double interval_width =
98 (splits == 0 || std::fabs(node_range) < std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) ? 1.0 : node_range / splits;
99
100 int pos = 0;
101 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
102 if (std::fabs(interval_width) < std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
103 pos = 0;
104 } else {
105 pos = std::clamp(static_cast<int>(std::floor((resource_values_vec[r] - node_lb) / interval_width)), 0,
106 splits - 1);
107 }
108 } else {
109 if (std::fabs(interval_width) < std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
110 pos = 0;
111 } else {
112 pos = std::clamp(static_cast<int>(std::floor((node_ub - resource_values_vec[r]) / interval_width)), 0,
113 splits - 1);
114 }
115 }
116
117 bucket_number += pos * multiplier;
118 multiplier *= splits;
119 }
120
121 // check if buckets[bucket_number] contains the resource_vec
122 if (unlikely(!buckets[bucket_number].contains(resource_values_vec))) {
123 // print resource_values_vec
124 for (auto val : resource_values_vec) { fmt::print("{}\n", val); }
125 // print bucket bounds
126 fmt::print("{}\n", buckets[bucket_number].lb[0]);
127 fmt::print("{}\n", buckets[bucket_number].ub[0]);
128
129 std::throw_with_nested(std::runtime_error("Resource values not contained in bucket"));
130 return -1;
131 }
132
133 return bucket_number;
134}

◆ get_opposite_bucket_number()

template<Direction D>
int BucketGraph::get_opposite_bucket_number ( int current_bucket_index,
std::vector< double > & inc )

Get the opposite bucket number for a given bucket index.

This function retrieves the opposite bucket number based on the current bucket index and the specified direction. It determines the node and bounds of the current bucket, then calculates the opposite bucket index using the appropriate direction.

Definition at line 409 of file BucketUtils.h.

409 {
410 // Retrieve the current bucket based on the current direction.
411 const auto &current_bucket =
412 (D == Direction::Forward) ? fw_buckets[current_bucket_index] : bw_buckets[current_bucket_index];
413 const int node = current_bucket.node_id;
414 const auto &theNode = nodes[node];
415
416 // Pre-compute the reference point for the given node using the main
417 // resources.
418 const size_t num_resources = options.main_resources.size();
419 // Optionally, add an assertion or check that inc has the expected size.
420 assert(inc.size() == num_resources && theNode.lb.size() == num_resources && theNode.ub.size() == num_resources);
421
422 std::vector<double> reference_point(num_resources);
423
424 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
425 // For Forward direction:
426 // Use the maximum of inc and the node's lower bounds.
427 // This ensures we do not fall below the allowed lower bound.
428 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_resources; ++i) {
429 reference_point[i] = std::max(inc[i], theNode.lb[i]);
430 // Optionally, adjust with epsilon if needed:
431 // reference_point[i] += numericutils::eps;
432 }
433 // Get the opposite bucket using the Backward tree.
434 return get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(node, reference_point);
435 } else {
436 // For Backward direction:
437 // Use the minimum of inc and the node's upper bounds.
438 // This ensures we do not exceed the allowed upper bound.
439 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_resources; ++i) {
440 reference_point[i] = std::min(inc[i], theNode.ub[i]);
441 // Optionally, adjust with epsilon if needed:
442 // reference_point[i] -= numericutils::eps;
443 }
444 // Get the opposite bucket using the Forward tree.
445 return get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(node, reference_point);
446 }
447}

◆ heuristic_fixing()

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::heuristic_fixing ( )

Applies heuristic fixing to the current solution.

This function modifies the current solution based on the heuristic fixing strategy using the provided vector of values.

Definition at line 524 of file BucketUtils.h.

524 {
525 if (fixed) { return; }
526
527 // Reset and initialize state.
528 reset_pool();
529 reset_fixed();
530 common_initialization();
531
532 // Pre-allocate c-bar vectors with infinity.
533 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
534 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
535
536 gap = std::ceil(incumbent - (relaxation + std::min(0.0, min_red_cost)));
537
538 // Run the appropriate labeling algorithms based on stage
539 if (S == Stage::Three) {
540 // Run the labeling algorithms for Stage::Two with Full::Partial mode.
541 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Two, Full::Partial>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
542 } else if (S == Stage::Four) {
543 fmt::print("\033[34m_STARTING ARC FIXING PROCEDURE\033[0m\n");
544 fmt::print("gap: {}\n", gap);
545 // Run the labeling algorithms for Stage::Four with Full::Full mode.
546 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Four, Full::Full>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
547 }
548
549 // Prepare containers for grouping labels by node.
550 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
551
552 // Preallocate vectors with nullptr to avoid resizing
553 std::vector<const Label *> min_fw_labels(num_nodes, nullptr);
554 std::vector<const Label *> min_bw_labels(num_nodes, nullptr);
555
556 // Find minimum cost label for each node directly from buckets
557 // without creating intermediate label maps
558 for (auto &bucket : fw_buckets) {
559 for (const Label *label : bucket.get_labels()) {
560 if (label == nullptr || label->is_dominated) continue;
561 const size_t node_id = label->node_id;
562 if (!min_fw_labels[node_id] || label->cost < min_fw_labels[node_id]->cost) {
563 min_fw_labels[node_id] = label;
564 }
565 }
566 }
567
568 for (auto &bucket : bw_buckets) {
569 for (const Label *label : bucket.get_labels()) {
570 if (label == nullptr || label->is_dominated) continue;
571 const size_t node_id = label->node_id;
572 if (!min_bw_labels[node_id] || label->cost < min_bw_labels[node_id]->cost) {
573 min_bw_labels[node_id] = label;
574 }
575 }
576 }
577
578 // Pre-compute the index of the "time" resource.
579 const size_t time_index =
580 std::distance(options.resources.begin(), std::find(options.resources.begin(), options.resources.end(), "time"));
581
582 // Precompute lookup tables for common values
583 std::vector<double> fw_time_resources(num_nodes, 0.0);
584 std::vector<double> bw_time_resources(num_nodes, 0.0);
585 std::vector<double> fw_costs(num_nodes, 0.0);
586 std::vector<double> bw_costs(num_nodes, 0.0);
587
588 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
589 if (min_fw_labels[i]) {
590 fw_time_resources[i] = min_fw_labels[i]->resources[time_index];
591 fw_costs[i] = min_fw_labels[i]->cost;
592 }
593 if (min_bw_labels[i]) {
594 bw_time_resources[i] = min_bw_labels[i]->resources[time_index];
595 bw_costs[i] = min_bw_labels[i]->cost;
596 }
597 }
598
599 // Cache node data to avoid repeated lookups
600 std::vector<double> node_durations(num_nodes);
601 std::vector<std::vector<double>> node_consumptions(num_nodes, std::vector<double>(options.resources.size()));
602
603 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
604 const VRPNode &node = nodes[i];
605 node_durations[i] = node.duration;
606 for (size_t r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) { node_consumptions[i][r] = node.consumption[r]; }
607 }
608
609 size_t num_fixes = 0;
610 const double gap_threshold = gap; // Cache this value
611
612 // Build a list of candidate node pairs to check
613 struct NodePair {
614 size_t i;
615 size_t j;
616 };
617
618 std::vector<NodePair> candidate_pairs;
619 candidate_pairs.reserve(num_nodes * num_nodes / 4); // Estimate: 25% of pairs will be candidates
620
621 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
622 if (!min_fw_labels[i]) continue;
623
624 for (size_t j = 0; j < num_nodes; ++j) {
625 if (i == j || !min_bw_labels[j]) continue;
626 candidate_pairs.push_back({i, j});
627 }
628 }
629
630 // Process candidate pairs in batches to improve cache locality
631 const size_t BATCH_SIZE = 256;
632 std::vector<std::pair<size_t, size_t>> arcs_to_fix;
633 arcs_to_fix.reserve(BATCH_SIZE);
634
635 for (size_t start = 0; start < candidate_pairs.size(); start += BATCH_SIZE) {
636 const size_t end = std::min(start + BATCH_SIZE, candidate_pairs.size());
637 arcs_to_fix.clear();
638
639 for (size_t idx = start; idx < end; ++idx) {
640 const auto &pair = candidate_pairs[idx];
641 const size_t i = pair.i;
642 const size_t j = pair.j;
643
644 const double cost = getcij(min_fw_labels[i]->node_id, min_bw_labels[j]->node_id);
645
646 // Early rejection using precomputed time resources (often most
647 // restrictive)
648 if (options.resources[time_index] == "time") {
649 if (fw_time_resources[i] + cost + node_durations[min_fw_labels[i]->node_id] > bw_time_resources[j]) {
650 continue;
651 }
652 }
653
654 // Check all other resources
655 bool violated = false;
656 const size_t fw_node_id = min_fw_labels[i]->node_id;
657
658 for (size_t r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
659 if (r != time_index) {
660 if (min_fw_labels[i]->resources[r] + node_consumptions[fw_node_id][r] >
661 min_bw_labels[j]->resources[r]) {
662 violated = true;
663 break;
664 }
665 }
666 }
667
668 // If all resource constraints are satisfied and the cost exceeds
669 // the gap, fix the arc
670 if (!violated && (fw_costs[i] + cost + bw_costs[j] > gap_threshold)) {
671 arcs_to_fix.emplace_back(i, j);
672 ++num_fixes;
673 }
674 }
675
676 // Fix arcs in batch
677 for (const auto &[i, j] : arcs_to_fix) { fix_arc(i, j); }
678 }
679
680 if (S == Stage::Four) {
681 fmt::print("Applied {} arc fixes\n", num_fixes);
682 fmt::print("\033[34m_ARC FIXING PROCEDURE FINISHED\033[0m\n");
683 }
684}

◆ initInfo()

void BucketGraph::initInfo ( )
static

Prints the configuration information of the BucketGraph.

This function outputs the configuration details including resource size, number of clients, and maximum SRC cuts. It also conditionally prints whether RIH, RCC, and SRC are enabled or disabled based on the preprocessor directives.

The output format is as follows:

+-------------------------------—+ | CONFIGURATION INFO | +-------------------------------—+ Resources: <R_SIZE> Number of Clients: <N_SIZE> Maximum SRC cuts: <MAX_SRC_CUTS> RIH: <enabled/disabled> RCC: <enabled/disabled> SRC: <enabled/disabled> +-------------------------------—+

Definition at line 978 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

978 {
979 // Print header
980 fmt::print("\n+----------------------------------+\n");
981 fmt::print("| CONFIGURATION INFO |\n");
982 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
983
984 // Print Resource size
985 fmt::print("Resources: {}\n", R_SIZE);
986 fmt::print("Number of Clients: {}\n", N_SIZE - 2);
987
988 // Conditional configuration (RIH enabled/disabled)
989#ifdef RIH
990 fmt::print("RIH: enabled\n");
991#else
992 fmt::print("RIH: disabled\n");
993#endif
994#ifdef RCC
995 fmt::print("RCC: enabled\n");
996#else
997 fmt::print("RCC: disabled\n");
998#endif
999#ifdef SRC
1000 fmt::print("SRC: enabled\n");
1001#else
1002 fmt::print("SRC: disabled\n");
1003#endif
1004 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
1005
1006 fmt::print("\n");
1007}

1193#endif

Checks if a node is unreachable based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a node, identified by its node_id, is unreachable by checking a specific bit in a bitmap. The bitmap is represented as an array of 64-bit unsigned integers.

bool BucketGraph::is_node_visited ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,

inlinestatic

Checks if a node has been visited based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a specific node, identified by node_id, has been visited by checking the corresponding bit in a bitmap array. The bitmap is an array of 64-bit unsigned integers, where each bit represents the visited status of a node.

89 const auto &sorted_sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs_sorted, bw_sccs_sorted);
90 const auto &n_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
91 auto &stat_n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
92 auto &stat_n_dom = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
93 auto &arc_scores = assign_buckets<D>(fw_arc_scores, bw_arc_scores);
94 auto &best_label = assign_buckets<D>(fw_best_label, bw_best_label);
95
96 const bool profile_labeling = options.profile_labeling;
97 const double src_compensation_bound =
98 (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) ? src_cost_compensation_bound() : 0.0;
99
100 // Reset the total label count.
101 n_labels = 0;
102
103 // Pre-calculate bitmap segments for dominance-checking.
104 const size_t n_segments = (n_buckets + 63) >> 6;
105 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
106 std::vector<uint32_t> touched_segments;
107 touched_segments.reserve(std::min<size_t>(n_segments, 64));
108 std::vector<int> bucket_pos_in_scc(n_buckets, -1);
109
110 // Process only newly queued labels while preserving the bucket order
111 // inside each SCC.
112 std::vector<std::vector<Label *>> pending_labels_by_bucket;
113 std::vector<size_t> pending_label_cursor;
114 std::vector<uint8_t> bucket_is_active;
115 std::vector<int> active_bucket_heap;
116
117 // Precompute main resource index and q_star value for partial solutions.
118 constexpr bool is_partial = (F == Full::Partial);
119 int main_res_index = 0;
120 double q_star_value = 0.0;
121 if constexpr (is_partial) {
122 main_res_index = options.main_resources[0];
123 q_star_value = std::floor(q_star[main_res_index]);
124 }
125
126 // Reuse a thread-local vector for collecting labels to dominate.
127 static thread_local std::vector<Label *> tl_labels_to_dominate;
128 tl_labels_to_dominate.clear();
129 tl_labels_to_dominate.reserve(64); // Pre-reserve space to avoid reallocations
130
131 // Process each strongly connected component (SCC) in topological order.
132 for (const auto &scc_index : topological_order) {
133 const auto &scc_buckets = sorted_sccs[scc_index];
134 if (scc_buckets.empty()) continue;
135
136 pending_labels_by_bucket.clear();
137 pending_labels_by_bucket.resize(scc_buckets.size());
138 pending_label_cursor.assign(scc_buckets.size(), 0);
139 bucket_is_active.assign(scc_buckets.size(), 0);
140 active_bucket_heap.clear();
141
142 auto activate_bucket = [&](int bucket_pos) {
143 if (bucket_pos < 0 || bucket_is_active[bucket_pos]) return;
144 bucket_is_active[bucket_pos] = 1;
145 active_bucket_heap.push_back(bucket_pos);
146 std::push_heap(active_bucket_heap.begin(), active_bucket_heap.end(), std::greater<int>());
147 };
148
149 for (size_t bucket_idx = 0; bucket_idx < scc_buckets.size(); ++bucket_idx) {
150 const int bucket = scc_buckets[bucket_idx];
151 bucket_pos_in_scc[bucket] = static_cast<int>(bucket_idx);
152
153 const auto &bucket_labels = buckets[bucket].get_sorted_labels();
154 const auto &extra_labels = buckets[bucket].get_extra_labels();
155 auto &pending_labels = pending_labels_by_bucket[bucket_idx];
156 pending_labels.reserve(bucket_labels.size() + extra_labels.size());
157
158 for (Label *label : bucket_labels) {
159 if (!label->is_extended && !label->is_dominated) pending_labels.push_back(label);
160 }
161 for (Label *label : extra_labels) {
162 if (!label->is_extended && !label->is_dominated) pending_labels.push_back(label);
163 }
164
165 if (!pending_labels.empty()) activate_bucket(static_cast<int>(bucket_idx));
166 }
167
168 while (!active_bucket_heap.empty()) {
169 std::pop_heap(active_bucket_heap.begin(), active_bucket_heap.end(), std::greater<int>());
170 const int bucket_idx = active_bucket_heap.back();
171 active_bucket_heap.pop_back();
172
173 const int bucket = scc_buckets[bucket_idx];
174 auto &pending_labels = pending_labels_by_bucket[bucket_idx];
175 size_t &pending_cursor = pending_label_cursor[bucket_idx];
176
177 while (pending_cursor < pending_labels.size()) {
178 Label *label = pending_labels[pending_cursor++];
179
180 // Skip labels already processed.
181 if (__builtin_expect(label->is_extended || label->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
182
183 // For partial solutions, check resource feasibility.
184 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP && F != Full::TSP) {
185 if constexpr (is_partial) {
186 const auto main_resource = label->resources[main_res_index];
187 if ((D == Direction::Forward && main_resource > q_star_value) ||
188 (D == Direction::Backward && main_resource <= q_star_value)) {
189 label->set_extended(true);
190 continue;
191 }
192 }
193 }
194
195 for (uint32_t segment_idx : touched_segments) { Bvisited[segment_idx] = 0; }
196 touched_segments.clear();
197
198 // Check if the label is dominated by labels in smaller
199 // buckets.
200 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
201 if (profile_labeling) profile_record_dominance_check(D, S);
202 if (DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets<D, S>(label, bucket, c_bar, Bvisited, touched_segments,
203 stat_n_dom))
204 continue;
205 }
206
207 // Retrieve outgoing arcs for the current label.
208 const auto &node_arcs = buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
209 if (__builtin_expect(node_arcs.empty(), 0)) {
210 label->set_extended(true);
211 continue;
212 }
213
214 // Prefetch first few arcs
215 if (!node_arcs.empty()) {
216 for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(size_t(4), node_arcs.size()); ++i) {
217 __builtin_prefetch(&node_arcs[i], 0, 3);
218 }
219 }
220
221 // Extend first, then process labels grouped by destination
222 // bucket. This keeps the destination bucket and its SoA cache
223 // hot across the whole group and amortizes staged-tier merges.
224 static thread_local std::vector<Label *> destination_batch;
225 destination_batch.clear();
226 destination_batch.reserve(node_arcs.size());
227 for (size_t arc_idx = 0; arc_idx < node_arcs.size(); ++arc_idx) {
228 const auto &arc = node_arcs[arc_idx];
229
230 // Prefetch next arc if available
231 if (arc_idx + 4 < node_arcs.size()) { __builtin_prefetch(&node_arcs[arc_idx + 4], 0, 3); }
232
233 auto new_label = Extend<D, S, ArcType::Bucket, Mutability::Mut, F>(label, arc);
234 if (new_label != nullptr) destination_batch.push_back(new_label);
235 }
236
237 std::stable_sort(destination_batch.begin(), destination_batch.end(), [](const Label *a, const Label *b) {
238 return a->vertex < b->vertex;
239 });
240
241 // Remove dominance among sibling extensions before scanning
242 // the committed destination bucket. Labels are grouped by
243 // destination, so each pair here is directly comparable.
244 for (size_t group_begin = 0; group_begin < destination_batch.size();) {
245 size_t group_end = group_begin + 1;
246 while (group_end < destination_batch.size() &&
247 destination_batch[group_end]->vertex == destination_batch[group_begin]->vertex) {
248 ++group_end;
249 }
250
251 for (size_t i = group_begin; i < group_end; ++i) {
252 Label *lhs = destination_batch[i];
253 if (lhs->is_dominated) continue;
254 for (size_t j = i + 1; j < group_end; ++j) {
255 Label *rhs = destination_batch[j];
256 if (rhs->is_dominated) continue;
257 if constexpr (S == Stage::One) {
258 if (lhs->cost <= rhs->cost) {
259 rhs->set_dominated(true);
260 } else {
261 lhs->set_dominated(true);
262 break;
263 }
264 } else {
265 if (is_dominated<D, S>(rhs, lhs)) rhs->set_dominated(true);
266 if (!rhs->is_dominated && is_dominated<D, S>(lhs, rhs)) {
267 lhs->set_dominated(true);
268 break;
269 }
270 }
271 }
272 }
273 group_begin = group_end;
274 }
275
276 for (Label *new_label : destination_batch) {
277 // Process each new label produced by the extension.
278 if (new_label != nullptr) {
279 if (new_label->is_dominated) continue;
280 if constexpr (S == Stage::Enumerate) {
281 if (n_labels >= enumeration_policy.max_labels_per_direction) {
282 enumeration_failed.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
283 label->set_extended(true);
284 continue;
285 }
286 }
287 ++stat_n_labels;
288 const int to_bucket = new_label->vertex;
289 auto &mother_bucket = buckets[to_bucket];
290 uint64_t bucket_scan_count = 0;
291
292 if (profile_labeling) { profile_record_new_label(D, S); }
293
294 // Prefetch the mother bucket and its labels
295 __builtin_prefetch(&mother_bucket, 0, 3);
296
297 mother_bucket.flush_extra_labels_if_large();
298 const auto &to_bucket_labels = mother_bucket.get_sorted_labels();
299 const auto &to_bucket_extra = mother_bucket.get_extra_labels();
300 constexpr bool uses_visited_dominance =
301 S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate;
302 const uint64_t new_visited_signature = new_label->visited_signature();
303#if defined(BALDES_HAS_SIMD)
304 if constexpr (uses_visited_dominance) {
305 if (!to_bucket_labels.empty()) mother_bucket.ensure_label_cache();
306 }
307#endif
308
309 // Prefetch first few destination bucket labels
310 if (!to_bucket_labels.empty()) {
311 for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(size_t(4), to_bucket_labels.size()); ++i) {
312 __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i], 0, 3);
313 }
314 }
315
316 bool dominated = false;
317 if constexpr (S == Stage::One) {
318 // Stage One: mark higher-cost labels as
319 // dominated.
320 for (auto *existing_label : to_bucket_labels) {
321 ++bucket_scan_count;
322 if (existing_label->is_dominated) continue;
323 if (new_label->cost < existing_label->cost)
324 existing_label->set_dominated(true);
325 else {
326 dominated = true;
327 break;
328 }
329 }
330 if (!dominated) {
331 for (auto *existing_label : to_bucket_extra) {
332 ++bucket_scan_count;
333 if (existing_label->is_dominated) continue;
334 if (new_label->cost < existing_label->cost)
335 existing_label->set_dominated(true);
336 else {
337 dominated = true;
338 break;
339 }
340 }
341 }
342 if (profile_labeling) {
343 profile_record_dominance_check(D, S);
344 profile_record_inner_bin_scan(D, S, bucket_scan_count);
345 if (D == Direction::Forward) {
346 dominance_checks_per_bucket[to_bucket] += static_cast<int>(bucket_scan_count);
347 }
348 }
349 } else {
350 uint64_t inner_scan_count = 0;
351
352 // RC-bracketed single-pass dominance (RouteOpt
353 // dominance.hpp doDominance style). The committed
354 // tier is RC-sorted; for non-SRC stages, raw cost
355 // decides which dominance direction is possible:
356 // cur.cost < new.cost - eps: only cur can
357 // dominate new, so test resource/path dominance
358 // of new by cur; stop if true.
359 // cur.cost > new.cost + eps: only new can
360 // dominate cur, so test resource/path dominance
361 // of cur by new; mark cur dominated if true.
362 // near-equal costs: test both full predicates.
363 // SRC compensation can overturn raw cost order, so
364 // widen the bracket by a conservative bound: the
365 // sum of absolute active SRC duals. Outside this
366 // interval only one dominance direction is
367 // possible; inside it the full SRC predicate is
368 // evaluated in both directions.
369 const double cost_lo =
370 new_label->cost - src_compensation_bound - numericutils::eps;
371 const double cost_hi =
372 new_label->cost + src_compensation_bound + numericutils::eps;
373
374 auto label_cost_less = [](const Label *label, double value) { return label->cost < value; };
375
376 auto scan_new_dominated_by = [&](size_t begin, size_t end) {
377#if defined(BALDES_HAS_SIMD)
378 {
379 namespace stdx = std::experimental;
380 using simd_d = stdx::native_simd<double>;
381 using mask_d = typename simd_d::mask_type;
382
383 constexpr size_t simd_width = simd_d::size();
384 const simd_d eps(numericutils::eps);
385 size_t i = begin;
386 for (; i + simd_width <= end; i += simd_width) {
387 mask_d candidate(true);
388 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
389 for (size_t r = 1; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
390 const simd_d res(mother_bucket.soa_resources[r].data() + i,
391 stdx::element_aligned);
392 candidate &= res <= (simd_d(new_label->resources[r]) + eps);
393 }
394 if (!options.resources.empty()) {
395 const simd_d res0(mother_bucket.soa_resources[0].data() + i,
396 stdx::element_aligned);
397 candidate &= res0 <= (simd_d(new_label->resources[0]) + eps);
398 }
399 } else {
400 for (size_t r = 1; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
401 const simd_d res(mother_bucket.soa_resources[r].data() + i,
402 stdx::element_aligned);
403 candidate &= res >= (simd_d(new_label->resources[r]) - eps);
404 }
405 if (!options.resources.empty()) {
406 const simd_d res0(mother_bucket.soa_resources[0].data() + i,
407 stdx::element_aligned);
408 candidate &= res0 >= (simd_d(new_label->resources[0]) - eps);
409 }
410 }
411 if (!stdx::any_of(candidate)) {
412 inner_scan_count += simd_width;
413 continue;
414 }
415 for (size_t lane = 0; lane < simd_width; ++lane) {
416 ++inner_scan_count;
417 if (!candidate[lane]) continue;
418 if constexpr (uses_visited_dominance) {
419 if ((mother_bucket.soa_visited_signatures[i + lane] &
420 ~new_visited_signature) != 0) {
421 if (profile_labeling) profile_record_signature_rejection(D, S);
422 continue;
423 }
424 }
425 Label *cur = to_bucket_labels[i + lane];
426 if (__builtin_expect(cur->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
427 if (dominates_resource_path<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
428 ++stat_n_dom;
429 dominated = true;
430 return;
431 }
432 }
433 }
434 begin = i;
435 }
436#endif
437 for (size_t i = begin; i < end; ++i) {
438 if (i + 8 < end) { __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i + 8], 0, 3); }
439 Label *cur = to_bucket_labels[i];
440 ++inner_scan_count;
441 if (__builtin_expect(cur->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
442 if (dominates_resource_path<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
443 ++stat_n_dom;
444 dominated = true;
445 break;
446 }
447 }
448 };
449
450 auto scan_existing_dominated_by_new = [&](size_t begin, size_t end) {
451#if defined(BALDES_HAS_SIMD)
452 {
453 namespace stdx = std::experimental;
454 using simd_d = stdx::native_simd<double>;
455 using mask_d = typename simd_d::mask_type;
456
457 constexpr size_t simd_width = simd_d::size();
458 const simd_d eps(numericutils::eps);
459 size_t i = begin;
460 for (; i + simd_width <= end; i += simd_width) {
461 mask_d candidate(true);
462 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
463 for (size_t r = 1; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
464 const simd_d res(mother_bucket.soa_resources[r].data() + i,
465 stdx::element_aligned);
466 candidate &= res >= (simd_d(new_label->resources[r]) - eps);
467 }
468 if (!options.resources.empty()) {
469 const simd_d res0(mother_bucket.soa_resources[0].data() + i,
470 stdx::element_aligned);
471 candidate &= res0 >= (simd_d(new_label->resources[0]) - eps);
472 }
473 } else {
474 for (size_t r = 1; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
475 const simd_d res(mother_bucket.soa_resources[r].data() + i,
476 stdx::element_aligned);
477 candidate &= res <= (simd_d(new_label->resources[r]) + eps);
478 }
479 if (!options.resources.empty()) {
480 const simd_d res0(mother_bucket.soa_resources[0].data() + i,
481 stdx::element_aligned);
482 candidate &= res0 <= (simd_d(new_label->resources[0]) + eps);
483 }
484 }
485 if (!stdx::any_of(candidate)) {
486 inner_scan_count += simd_width;
487 continue;
488 }
489 for (size_t lane = 0; lane < simd_width; ++lane) {
490 ++inner_scan_count;
491 if (!candidate[lane]) continue;
492 if constexpr (uses_visited_dominance) {
493 if ((new_visited_signature &
494 ~mother_bucket.soa_visited_signatures[i + lane]) != 0) {
495 if (profile_labeling) profile_record_signature_rejection(D, S);
496 continue;
497 }
498 }
499 Label *cur = to_bucket_labels[i + lane];
500 if (__builtin_expect(cur->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
501 if (dominates_resource_path<D, S>(cur, new_label)) {
502 cur->set_dominated(true);
503 }
504 }
505 }
506 begin = i;
507 }
508#endif
509 for (size_t i = begin; i < end; ++i) {
510 if (i + 8 < end) { __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i + 8], 0, 3); }
511 Label *cur = to_bucket_labels[i];
512 ++inner_scan_count;
513 if (__builtin_expect(cur->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
514 if (dominates_resource_path<D, S>(cur, new_label)) { cur->set_dominated(true); }
515 }
516 };
517
518 auto scan_bracket = [&](size_t begin, size_t end) {
519#if defined(BALDES_HAS_SIMD)
520 {
521 namespace stdx = std::experimental;
522 using simd_d = stdx::native_simd<double>;
523 using mask_d = typename simd_d::mask_type;
524
525 constexpr size_t simd_width = simd_d::size();
526 const simd_d eps(numericutils::eps);
527 size_t i = begin;
528 for (; i + simd_width <= end; i += simd_width) {
529 mask_d cur_dominates_new(true);
530 mask_d new_dominates_cur(true);
531 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
532 for (size_t r = 1; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
533 const simd_d res(mother_bucket.soa_resources[r].data() + i,
534 stdx::element_aligned);
535 cur_dominates_new &= res <= (simd_d(new_label->resources[r]) + eps);
536 new_dominates_cur &= res >= (simd_d(new_label->resources[r]) - eps);
537 }
538 if (!options.resources.empty()) {
539 const simd_d res0(mother_bucket.soa_resources[0].data() + i,
540 stdx::element_aligned);
541 cur_dominates_new &= res0 <= (simd_d(new_label->resources[0]) + eps);
542 new_dominates_cur &= res0 >= (simd_d(new_label->resources[0]) - eps);
543 }
544 } else {
545 for (size_t r = 1; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
546 const simd_d res(mother_bucket.soa_resources[r].data() + i,
547 stdx::element_aligned);
548 cur_dominates_new &= res >= (simd_d(new_label->resources[r]) - eps);
549 new_dominates_cur &= res <= (simd_d(new_label->resources[r]) + eps);
550 }
551 if (!options.resources.empty()) {
552 const simd_d res0(mother_bucket.soa_resources[0].data() + i,
553 stdx::element_aligned);
554 cur_dominates_new &= res0 >= (simd_d(new_label->resources[0]) - eps);
555 new_dominates_cur &= res0 <= (simd_d(new_label->resources[0]) + eps);
556 }
557 }
558 const mask_d candidate = cur_dominates_new | new_dominates_cur;
559 if (!stdx::any_of(candidate)) {
560 inner_scan_count += simd_width;
561 continue;
562 }
563 for (size_t lane = 0; lane < simd_width; ++lane) {
564 ++inner_scan_count;
565 if (!candidate[lane]) continue;
566 if constexpr (uses_visited_dominance) {
567 if (cur_dominates_new[lane] &&
568 (mother_bucket.soa_visited_signatures[i + lane] &
569 ~new_visited_signature) != 0) {
570 cur_dominates_new[lane] = false;
571 if (profile_labeling) profile_record_signature_rejection(D, S);
572 }
573 if (new_dominates_cur[lane] &&
574 (new_visited_signature &
575 ~mother_bucket.soa_visited_signatures[i + lane]) != 0) {
576 new_dominates_cur[lane] = false;
577 if (profile_labeling) profile_record_signature_rejection(D, S);
578 }
579 if (!cur_dominates_new[lane] && !new_dominates_cur[lane]) continue;
580 }
581 Label *cur = to_bucket_labels[i + lane];
582 if (__builtin_expect(cur->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
583 if (cur_dominates_new[lane] && is_dominated<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
584 ++stat_n_dom;
585 dominated = true;
586 return;
587 }
588 if (new_dominates_cur[lane] && is_dominated<D, S>(cur, new_label)) {
589 cur->set_dominated(true);
590 }
591 }
592 }
593 begin = i;
594 }
595#endif
596 for (size_t i = begin; i < end; ++i) {
597 if (i + 8 < end) { __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i + 8], 0, 3); }
598 Label *cur = to_bucket_labels[i];
599 ++inner_scan_count;
600 if (__builtin_expect(cur->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
601 if (is_dominated<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
602 ++stat_n_dom;
603 dominated = true;
604 break;
605 }
606 if (is_dominated<D, S>(cur, new_label)) { cur->set_dominated(true); }
607 }
608 };
609
610 // Forward path: walk sorted committed labels once.
611 const size_t to_bucket_size = to_bucket_labels.size();
612 {
613 static constexpr size_t kLinearCostBracketScanLimit = 32;
614 if (to_bucket_size <= kLinearCostBracketScanLimit) {
615 for (size_t i = 0; i < to_bucket_size; ++i) {
616 if (i + 8 < to_bucket_size) {
617 __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i + 8], 0, 3);
618 }
619 Label *cur = to_bucket_labels[i];
620 ++inner_scan_count;
621 if (__builtin_expect(cur->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
622 const double cur_cost = cur->cost;
623 if (cur_cost < cost_lo) {
624 if (dominates_resource_path<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
625 ++stat_n_dom;
626 dominated = true;
627 break;
628 }
629 } else if (cur_cost > cost_hi) {
630 if (dominates_resource_path<D, S>(cur, new_label)) {
631 cur->set_dominated(true);
632 }
633 } else {
634 if (is_dominated<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
635 ++stat_n_dom;
636 dominated = true;
637 break;
638 }
639 if (is_dominated<D, S>(cur, new_label)) { cur->set_dominated(true); }
640 }
641 }
642 } else {
643#if defined(BALDES_HAS_SIMD)
644 mother_bucket.ensure_label_cache();
645#endif
646 const auto bracket_begin_it = std::lower_bound(
647 to_bucket_labels.begin(), to_bucket_labels.end(), cost_lo, label_cost_less);
648 const auto expensive_begin_it = std::upper_bound(
649 to_bucket_labels.begin(), to_bucket_labels.end(), cost_hi,
650 [](double value, const Label *label) { return value < label->cost; });
651 const size_t bracket_begin =
652 static_cast<size_t>(std::distance(to_bucket_labels.begin(), bracket_begin_it));
653 const size_t expensive_begin = static_cast<size_t>(
654 std::distance(to_bucket_labels.begin(), expensive_begin_it));
655
656 scan_new_dominated_by(0, bracket_begin);
657 if (!dominated) { scan_bracket(bracket_begin, expensive_begin); }
658 if (!dominated) { scan_existing_dominated_by_new(expensive_begin, to_bucket_size); }
659 }
660 }
661
662 // Extra (unsorted) tier: same trichotomy without
663 // sort assumptions. Only walked if not dominated
664 // by the sorted tier.
665 if (!dominated) {
666 for (Label *cur : to_bucket_extra) {
667 ++inner_scan_count;
668 if (__builtin_expect(cur->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
669 const double cur_cost = cur->cost;
670 if (cur_cost < cost_lo) {
671 if (dominates_resource_path<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
672 ++stat_n_dom;
673 dominated = true;
674 break;
675 }
676 } else if (cur_cost > cost_hi) {
677 if (dominates_resource_path<D, S>(cur, new_label)) { cur->set_dominated(true); }
678 } else {
679 if (is_dominated<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
680 ++stat_n_dom;
681 dominated = true;
682 break;
683 }
684 if (is_dominated<D, S>(cur, new_label)) { cur->set_dominated(true); }
685 }
686 }
687 }
688 if (profile_labeling) {
689 profile_record_dominance_check(D, S);
690 profile_record_inner_bin_scan(D, S, inner_scan_count);
691 if (D == Direction::Forward) {
692 dominance_checks_per_bucket[to_bucket] += static_cast<int>(inner_scan_count);
693 }
694 }
695 }
696
697 if (!dominated) {
698 if (profile_labeling) profile_record_non_dominated_label(D, S);
699 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
700 ++non_dominated_labels_per_bucket;
701 } else {
702 ++non_dominated_labels_per_bucket_bw;
703 }
704 ++n_labels;
705#ifdef SORTED_LABELS
706 mother_bucket.add_sorted_label(new_label);
707#elif defined(LIMITED_BUCKETS)
708 mother_bucket.sorted_label(new_label, BUCKET_CAPACITY);
709#else
710 mother_bucket.add_label(new_label);
711#endif
712 const int to_bucket_pos = bucket_pos_in_scc[to_bucket];
713 if (to_bucket_pos != -1) {
714 pending_labels_by_bucket[to_bucket_pos].push_back(new_label);
715 activate_bucket(to_bucket_pos);
716 }
717 }
718 }
719 }
720 label->set_extended(true);
721 }
722
723 bucket_is_active[bucket_idx] = 0;
724 }
725
726 for (const int bucket : scc_buckets) {
727 bucket_pos_in_scc[bucket] = -1;
728 buckets[bucket].compact_dominated_labels();
729 }
730
731 // Update c_bar values for all buckets in the current SCC. The topology
732 // builder already stores these buckets in direction-specific resource
733 // order, so avoid copying and sorting them on every labeling pass.
734 const auto &sorted_buckets = sorted_sccs[scc_index];
735
736 // Prefetch data for c_bar updates
737 if (!sorted_buckets.empty()) {
738 for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(size_t(4), sorted_buckets.size()); ++i) {
739 __builtin_prefetch(&buckets[sorted_buckets[i]], 0, 3);
740 __builtin_prefetch(&Phi[sorted_buckets[i]], 0, 3);
741 }
742 }
743
744 // Batch processing of c_bar updates for improved cache locality.
745 for (size_t i = 0; i < sorted_buckets.size(); ++i) {
746 const int bucket = sorted_buckets[i];
747
748 // Prefetch data for next iterations
749 if (i + 4 < sorted_buckets.size()) {
750 __builtin_prefetch(&buckets[sorted_buckets[i + 4]], 0, 3);
751 __builtin_prefetch(&Phi[sorted_buckets[i + 4]], 0, 3);
752 }
753
754 double min_c_bar = buckets[bucket].get_cb();
755 const auto &phi_buckets = Phi[bucket];
756
757 // Prefetch first few phi buckets
758 if (!phi_buckets.empty()) {
759 for (size_t j = 0; j < std::min(size_t(4), phi_buckets.size()); ++j) {
760 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j]], 0, 3);
761 }
762 }
763
764 // Process phi buckets in blocks of 4 for better cache efficiency
765 size_t j = 0;
766 for (; j + 3 < phi_buckets.size(); j += 4) {
767 // Prefetch next block
768 if (j + 8 < phi_buckets.size()) {
769 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 4]], 0, 3);
770 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 5]], 0, 3);
771 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 6]], 0, 3);
772 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 7]], 0, 3);
773 }
774
775 // Compute min across 4 values at once
776 double val0 = c_bar[phi_buckets[j]];
777 double val1 = c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 1]];
778 double val2 = c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 2]];
779 double val3 = c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 3]];
780
781 min_c_bar = std::min(min_c_bar, std::min(std::min(val0, val1), std::min(val2, val3)));
782 }
783
784 // Process remaining phi buckets
785 for (; j < phi_buckets.size(); ++j) { min_c_bar = std::min(min_c_bar, c_bar[phi_buckets[j]]); }
786
787 c_bar[bucket] = min_c_bar;
788 }
789 }
790
791 // Store the best label from the labeling process.
792 if (Label *best_label_run = get_best_label<D>(topological_order, c_bar, sccs)) best_label = best_label_run;
793
794 return c_bar;
795}

◆ mono_initialization()

void BucketGraph::mono_initialization ( )

Initializes the BucketGraph for the mono-directional case.

584 {
585 // Clear previous data
586 merged_labels.clear();
587 merged_labels.reserve(100);
588 fw_c_bar.clear();
589 bw_c_bar.clear();
590
591 dominance_checks_per_bucket.assign(fw_buckets_size + 1, 0);
592 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket = 0;
593
594 // Resize cost vectors to match the number of buckets
595 fw_c_bar.resize(fw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
596 bw_c_bar.resize(bw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
597
598 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
599 auto &num_bucket_index = assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
600
601 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
602 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
603 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
604
605 auto &VRPNode = nodes[0]; // Example for the first node
606
607 // Calculate base intervals and total ranges for each resource dimension
608 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
609 base_intervals[r] = (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / intervals[r].interval;
610 }
611
612 // Clear forward and backward buckets
613 for (auto b = 0; b < fw_buckets.size(); b++) {
614 fw_buckets[b].clear();
615 bw_buckets[b].clear();
616 }
617
618 // Initialize forward buckets (generic for multiple dimensions)
619 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
620
621 std::vector<double> interval_starts(num_intervals);
622 std::vector<double> interval_ends(num_intervals);
623
624 // Initialize interval_starts to lower bounds and interval_ends to upper
625 // bounds
626 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
627 interval_starts[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
628 interval_ends[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
629 }
630 int offset_fw = 0;
631 int offset_bw = 0;
632
633 // Lambda to handle forward and backward direction initialization across
634 // combinations
635 auto initialize_intervals_combinations = [&](bool is_forward) {
636 int &offset = is_forward ? offset_fw : offset_bw;
637 auto &label_pool = is_forward ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
638 auto &buckets = is_forward ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
639 const auto &depot_id = is_forward ? options.depot : options.end_depot;
640 // print depot_id
641 int calculated_index_base =
642 is_forward ? num_buckets_index_fw[options.depot] : num_buckets_index_bw[options.end_depot];
643
644 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals,
645 0); // Tracks current position in each interval dimension
646 std::vector<double> interval_bounds(num_intervals);
647
648 // Recursive lambda to generate all combinations of intervals
649 std::function<void(int)> generate_combinations = [&](int depth) {
650 if (depth == num_intervals) {
651 // All dimensions processed, now initialize for the current
652 // combination
653 int calculated_index = calculated_index_base + offset;
654 auto depot = label_pool->acquire(calculated_index);
655
656 // Set interval_bounds to current combination of interval
657 // starts or ends
658 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
659 interval_bounds[r] =
660 is_forward ? interval_starts[r] + current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r])
661 : interval_ends[r] - current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r]);
662 }
663
664 // print interval
665 // Initialize depot with the current interval boundaries
666 depot->initialize(calculated_index, 0.0, interval_bounds, depot_id);
667 depot->is_extended = false;
668 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(depot_id);
669 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(depot_id);
670 depot->addNode(depot_id);
671 set_node_visited(depot->visited_bitmap, depot_id);
672 SRC_MODE_BLOCK(depot->SRCmap.assign(cut_storage->activeSize(), 0);)
673 buckets[calculated_index].add_label(depot);
674 buckets[calculated_index].node_id = depot_id;
675
676 offset++; // Increment offset for each combination
677 return;
678 }
679
680 // Loop through each interval in the current dimension
681 for (int k = 0; k < intervals[depth].interval; ++k) {
682 current_pos[depth] = k;
683 generate_combinations(depth + 1);
684 }
685 };
686
687 // Start generating combinations from depth 0
688 generate_combinations(0);
689 };
690
691 // Call the lambda for both forward and backward directions, ensuring
692 // all combinations are processed
693 initialize_intervals_combinations(true); // Forward direction
694}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ ObtainJumpBucketArcs()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::ObtainJumpBucketArcs ( )

Obtains jump bucket arcs for the BucketGraph.

This function iterates over each bucket in the BucketGraph and adds jump arcs to the set of buckets. It checks if each arc exists in the given Gamma vector for the current bucket. If an arc exists, it adds the corresponding bucket to the set of buckets to add jump arcs to. It then removes the non-component-wise minimal buckets from the set. Finally, it adds jump arcs from the current bucket to each bucket in the set.

Definition at line 355 of file BucketJump.h.

355 {
356 // Select direction-specific containers.
357 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
358 auto &num_buckets_idx = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
359 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
360 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
361 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
362
363 int arc_counter = 0; // Count of jump arcs added
364 int missing_counter = 0; // Count of missing paths
365
366 // Clear generated jump arcs from the previous jump-arc build. Jump arcs
367 // are also mirrored as marked BucketArc entries for the labeler.
368 for (auto &node : nodes) { node.clear_jump_arcs<D>(); }
369 for (auto &bucket : buckets) { bucket.template clear_generated_jump_arcs<D>(); }
370
371 // Bucket ids encode kappa in the paper's order. Jump arcs satisfy
372 // b_base \prec b_jump in kappa order for both senses. For backward buckets
373 // this means jumping to a numerically larger kappa / lower physical upper
374 // bound, with Extend applying q <- min(q, u_jump).
375 const int loop_begin = 0;
376 const int loop_end = buckets_size;
377 const int loop_step = 1;
378 for (int b = loop_begin; b != loop_end; b += loop_step) {
379 std::vector<int> B_bar; // Temporary storage for valid adjacent
380 // bucket indices
381
382 const int current_node_id = buckets[b].node_id;
383 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
384 const auto &orig_arcs = nodes[current_node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
385
386 // For each original arc from the current node...
387 for (const auto &orig_arc : orig_arcs) {
388 const int from_node = orig_arc.from;
389 const int to_node = orig_arc.to;
390 bool have_path = false;
391
392 // Check if the current bucket arcs contain the desired
393 // path.
394 for (const auto &gamma : bucket_arcs) {
395 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(gamma.from_bucket, gamma.to_bucket)) { continue; }
396
397 const int from_node_b = buckets[gamma.from_bucket].node_id;
398 const int to_node_b = buckets[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
399
400 if (from_node == from_node_b && to_node == to_node_b) {
401 have_path = true;
402 break; // Path found: no need to search further.
403 }
404 }
405
406 // If no valid path was found, try to obtain a jump arc.
407 if (!have_path) {
408 ++missing_counter;
409
410 // Determine bucket range for the current node.
411 const int start_bucket = num_buckets_idx[current_node_id];
412 const int node_buckets = num_buckets[current_node_id];
413 const auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits)[current_node_id];
414 const auto decode_pos = [&](int bucket_id) {
415 std::vector<int> pos(bucket_splits.size(), 0);
416 int local_bucket = bucket_id - start_bucket;
417 for (size_t r = 0; r < bucket_splits.size(); ++r) {
418 pos[r] = local_bucket % bucket_splits[r];
419 local_bucket /= bucket_splits[r];
420 }
421 return pos;
422 };
423 const auto current_pos = decode_pos(b);
424
425 auto is_jump_candidate = [&](const std::vector<int> &candidate_pos) {
426 bool strict = false;
427 for (size_t r = 0; r < candidate_pos.size(); ++r) {
428 if (candidate_pos[r] < current_pos[r]) return false;
429 if (candidate_pos[r] > current_pos[r]) strict = true;
430 }
431 return strict;
432 };
433
434 auto is_componentwise_closest = [&](const std::vector<int> &candidate_pos,
435 const std::vector<std::vector<int>> &all_positions) {
436 for (const auto &other_pos : all_positions) {
437 if (&other_pos == &candidate_pos) continue;
438 bool leq = true;
439 bool strict = false;
440 for (size_t r = 0; r < candidate_pos.size(); ++r) {
441 if (other_pos[r] > candidate_pos[r]) {
442 leq = false;
443 break;
444 }
445 if (other_pos[r] < candidate_pos[r]) strict = true;
446 }
447 if (leq && strict) return false;
448 }
449 return true;
450 };
451
452 std::vector<int> candidate_buckets;
453 std::vector<std::vector<int>> candidate_positions;
454 std::vector<std::array<double, R_SIZE>> candidate_res;
455 std::vector<double> candidate_costs;
456 candidate_buckets.reserve(node_buckets);
457
458 const int scan_begin = b + 1;
459 const int scan_end = start_bucket + node_buckets;
460 const int scan_step = 1;
461 for (int candidate_b = scan_begin; candidate_b != scan_end; candidate_b += scan_step) {
462 const auto candidate_pos = decode_pos(candidate_b);
463 if (!is_jump_candidate(candidate_pos)) continue;
464
465 const auto &candidate_arcs = buckets[candidate_b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
466 for (const auto &gamma_candidate : candidate_arcs) {
467 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(gamma_candidate.from_bucket, gamma_candidate.to_bucket)) continue;
468
469 const int from_node_candidate = buckets[gamma_candidate.from_bucket].node_id;
470 const int to_node_candidate = buckets[gamma_candidate.to_bucket].node_id;
471 if (from_node == from_node_candidate && to_node == to_node_candidate) {
472 candidate_buckets.push_back(candidate_b);
473 candidate_positions.push_back(candidate_pos);
474 candidate_res.push_back(gamma_candidate.resource_increment);
475 candidate_costs.push_back(gamma_candidate.cost_increment);
476 break;
477 }
478 }
479 }
480
481 // Keep the closest component-wise candidates: minimal
482 // kappa-larger buckets. This is the paper's B-dot front; it is
483 // direction-independent because backward buckets were already
484 // built as u_v - kappa*d.
485 for (size_t i = 0; i < candidate_buckets.size(); ++i) {
486 if (!is_componentwise_closest(candidate_positions[i], candidate_positions)) continue;
487
488 const int candidate_b = candidate_buckets[i];
489 B_bar.push_back(candidate_b);
490 std::vector<double> res(candidate_res[i].begin(),
491 candidate_res[i].begin() +
492 static_cast<std::ptrdiff_t>(options.main_resources.size()));
493 const double cost = candidate_costs[i];
494
495 buckets[b].template add_jump_arc<D>(b, candidate_b, res, cost);
496 nodes[buckets[b].node_id].template add_jump_arc<D>(b, candidate_b, res, cost, orig_arc.to);
497 buckets[b].template add_bucket_arc<D>(b, candidate_b, res, cost, true, orig_arc.to);
498 ++arc_counter;
499 }
500 }
501 }
502 }
503
504 // Print summary of jump arcs added.
505 CONDITIONAL(D, print_info("[Fw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter),
506 print_info("[Bw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter));
507}
CONDITIONAL
#define CONDITIONAL(D, FW_ACTION, BW_ACTION)

◆ print_statistics()

void BucketGraph::print_statistics ( )

Prints the statistics of the bucket graph.

This function outputs a formatted table displaying various metrics related to the bucket graph. The table includes headers and values for forward and backward labels, as well as dominance checks. The output is color-coded for better readability:

  • Bold blue for metric names
  • Green for values (backward labels)
  • Reset color to default after each line

The table structure: +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Metric | Forward | Backward | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Labels | <stat_n_labels_fw> | <stat_n_labels_bw> | | Dominance Check | <stat_n_dom_fw> | <stat_n_dom_bw> | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+

Note: The actual values for the metrics (e.g., stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw, etc.) should be provided by the corresponding member variables or functions.

Definition at line 730 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

730 {
731 const char *blue_bold = "\033[1;34m"; // Bold blue for metrics
732 const char *green = "\033[32m"; // Green for values (backward labels)
733 const char *reset = "\033[0m"; // Reset color
734
735 // Print table header with horizontal line and separators
736 fmt::print("\n+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
737 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Metric", reset, " Forward", " Backward");
738 fmt::print("+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
739
740 // Print labels for forward and backward with bold blue metric
741 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Labels", reset, stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
742
743 // Print dominated forward and backward labels with bold blue metric
744 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Dominance Check", reset, stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
745
746 // Print the final horizontal line
747 fmt::print("+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
748
749 if (options.profile_labeling) { print_labeling_profile(); }
750
751 fmt::print("\n");
752}

◆ process_all_resources()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma, typename VRPNode>
bool BucketGraph::process_all_resources ( std::vector< double > & new_resources,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t N )

Processes all resources by iterating through them and applying constraints.

This function recursively processes each resource in the new_resources vector by calling process_resource for each index from I to N-1. If any resource processing fails (i.e., process_resource returns false), the function returns false immediately. If all resources are processed successfully, the function returns true.

Definition at line 112 of file BucketRes.h.

114 {
115 for (size_t I = 0; I < N; ++I) {
116 switch (static_cast<ResourceType>(options.resource_type[I])) {
117 [[likely]] case ResourceType::Disposable:
118 if (!process_disposable_resource<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], gamma.resource_increment[I],
119 theNode.lb[I], theNode.ub[I])) {
120 return false;
121 }
122 break;
123
124 case ResourceType::NonDisposable:
125 if (!process_non_disposable_resource<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], gamma.resource_increment[I],
126 theNode.lb[I], theNode.ub[I])) {
127 return false;
128 }
129 break;
130
131 case ResourceType::Binary: new_resources[I] = process_binary_resource<D>(gamma.resource_increment[I]); break;
132
133 case ResourceType::Battery:
134 if (!process_battery_resource<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], theNode.is_station,
135 options.battery_capacity)) {
136 return false;
137 }
138 break;
139
140 case ResourceType::RechargeCount:
141 if (!process_recharge_count<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], theNode.is_station,
142 options.max_recharges)) {
143 return false;
144 }
145 break;
146
147 case ResourceType::RechargeTime:
148 if (!process_recharge_time<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], theNode.is_station,
149 options.max_recharge_time)) {
150 return false;
151 }
152 break;
153
154 case ResourceType::MTW:
155 if (!process_mtw_resource<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], gamma.resource_increment[I],
156 theNode)) {
157 return false;
158 }
159 break;
160 }
161 }
162 return true;
163}

◆ process_resource()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma, typename VRPNode>
bool BucketGraph::process_resource ( double & new_resource,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t I )
constexpr

Processes a resource based on its disposability type and direction.

This function updates the new_resource value based on the initial resources, the increment provided by gamma, and the constraints defined by theNode. The behavior varies depending on the disposability type of the resource.

double BucketGraph::relative_gap_to_incumbent ( double lower_bound) const
inlinenoexcept

642 {
643 if (!std::isfinite(incumbent) || !std::isfinite(lower_bound)) return std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
644 const double denom = std::max(1.0, std::abs(incumbent));
645 return std::max(0.0, incumbent - lower_bound) / denom;
646 }

◆ reset_fixed()

void BucketGraph::reset_fixed ( )
inline

Resets all fixed arcs in the graph.

This function iterates over each row in the fixed_arcs matrix and sets all elements to 0. It effectively clears any fixed arc constraints that may have been previously set.

◆ run_labeling_algorithms()

template<Stage state, Full fullness>
void BucketGraph::run_labeling_algorithms ( std::vector< double > & forward_cbar,
std::vector< double > & backward_cbar )

Runs forward and backward labeling algorithms in parallel and synchronizes the results.

T#his function creates tasks for forward and backward labeling algorithms using the provided scheduling mechanism. The tasks are executed in parallel, and the results are synchronized and stored in the provided vectors.

Definition at line 1522 of file BucketSolve.h.

1522 {
1523 if constexpr (state == Stage::Four || state == Stage::Enumerate) {
1524 ++completion_exact_calls;
1525 bool use_backward_first = false;
1526#if defined(BALDES_COMPLETION_SCHEDULE_BACKWARD_FIRST)
1527 use_backward_first = true;
1528#elif defined(BALDES_COMPLETION_SCHEDULE_PARALLEL)
1529 use_backward_first = false;
1530#else
1531 // AUTO starts parallel, probes backward-first every eight exact calls,
1532 // and periodically rechecks parallel even after choosing sequential.
1533 const uint64_t phase = completion_exact_calls % 8;
1534 use_backward_first = auto_prefer_backward_first ? phase != 0 : phase == 4;
1535#endif
1536
1537 if (use_backward_first) {
1538 completion_bound_attempts.store(0, std::memory_order_relaxed);
1539 completion_bound_rejections.store(0, std::memory_order_relaxed);
1540 const auto backward_start = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
1541 forward_completion_bounds_ready = false;
1542 backward_cbar = labeling_algorithm<Direction::Backward, state, fullness>();
1543 const auto backward_end = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
1544 bw_c_bar = backward_cbar;
1545 forward_completion_bounds_ready = true;
1546 forward_cbar = labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, state, fullness>();
1547 const auto forward_end = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
1548 forward_completion_bounds_ready = false;
1549
1550 const double backward_ms =
1551 std::chrono::duration<double, std::milli>(backward_end - backward_start).count();
1552 const double forward_ms =
1553 std::chrono::duration<double, std::milli>(forward_end - backward_end).count();
1554 last_sequential_completion_ms = backward_ms + forward_ms;
1555
1556#if defined(BALDES_COMPLETION_SCHEDULE_AUTO)
1557 const double attempts =
1558 static_cast<double>(completion_bound_attempts.load(std::memory_order_relaxed));
1559 const double rejected =
1560 static_cast<double>(completion_bound_rejections.load(std::memory_order_relaxed));
1561 const double rejection_ratio = attempts > 0.0 ? rejected / attempts : 0.0;
1562 const double estimated_unpruned_forward_ms = forward_ms / std::max(0.10, 1.0 - rejection_ratio);
1563 const double estimated_parallel_ms = std::max(backward_ms, estimated_unpruned_forward_ms);
1564 auto_prefer_backward_first = last_sequential_completion_ms < 0.95 * estimated_parallel_ms;
1565#endif
1566 return;
1567 }
1568 }
1569
1570 forward_completion_bounds_ready = false;
1571 double forward_ms = 0.0;
1572 double backward_ms = 0.0;
1573 // Schedule the forward labeling algorithm task on the scheduler.
1574 auto forward_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1575 const auto start = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
1576 auto result = labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, state, fullness>();
1577 forward_ms = std::chrono::duration<double, std::milli>(std::chrono::steady_clock::now() - start).count();
1578 return result;
1579 });
1580
1581 // Schedule the backward labeling algorithm task on the scheduler.
1582 auto backward_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1583 const auto start = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
1584 auto result = labeling_algorithm<Direction::Backward, state, fullness>();
1585 backward_ms = std::chrono::duration<double, std::milli>(std::chrono::steady_clock::now() - start).count();
1586 return result;
1587 });
1588
1589 // Combine the forward and backward tasks.
1590 auto combined_work = stdexec::when_all(std::move(forward_task), std::move(backward_task)) |
1591 stdexec::then([&](auto forward_result, auto backward_result) {
1592 // Store the results into the provided vectors.
1593 forward_cbar = std::move(forward_result);
1594 backward_cbar = std::move(backward_result);
1595 });
1596
1597 // Wait for all tasks to complete.
1598 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(combined_work));
1599 last_parallel_completion_ms = std::max(forward_ms, backward_ms);
1600#if defined(BALDES_COMPLETION_SCHEDULE_AUTO)
1601 if constexpr (state == Stage::Four || state == Stage::Enumerate) {
1602 if (completion_exact_calls % 8 == 0 && std::isfinite(last_sequential_completion_ms)) {
1603 auto_prefer_backward_first = last_sequential_completion_ms < 0.95 * last_parallel_completion_ms;
1604 }
1605 }
1606#endif
1607}

164 {

167 auto &bucket_graph = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_graph, bw_bucket_graph);
168
169 std::vector<std::vector<int>> extended_bucket_graph = bucket_graph;
170 if (extended_bucket_graph.size() < buckets.size()) { extended_bucket_graph.resize(buckets.size()); }
171 for (size_t i = 0; i < buckets.size(); ++i) {
172 const auto &phi_set = Phi[i];
173 if (phi_set.empty()) continue;
174 for (auto phi_bucket : phi_set) {
175 if (phi_bucket >= 0 && static_cast<size_t>(phi_bucket) < extended_bucket_graph.size()) {
176 extended_bucket_graph[static_cast<size_t>(phi_bucket)].push_back(static_cast<int>(i));
177 }
178 }
179 }
180
181 SCC scc_finder;
182 scc_finder.convertFromAdjacency(extended_bucket_graph);
183 auto sccs = scc_finder.tarjanSCC();
184 auto topological_order = scc_finder.topologicalOrderOfSCCs(sccs);
185
186#ifdef VERBOSE
187 constexpr auto blue = "\033[34m";
188 constexpr auto reset = "\033[0m";
189 fmt::print((D == Direction::Forward) ? "FW SCCs:\n" : "BW SCCs:\n");
190 for (auto scc : topological_order) {
191 fmt::print("{}({}) -> {}", blue, scc, reset);
192 for (auto bucket : sccs[scc]) { fmt::print("{} ", bucket); }
193 fmt::print("\n");
194 }
195 fmt::print("\n");
196#endif
197
198 std::vector<std::vector<int>> ordered_sccs;
199 ordered_sccs.reserve(sccs.size());
200 for (int scc : topological_order) { ordered_sccs.push_back(sccs[scc]); }
201
202 auto sorted_sccs = sccs;
203 for (auto &scc : sorted_sccs) {
204 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
205 pdqsort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) { return buckets[a].lb < buckets[b].lb; });
206 } else {
207 pdqsort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) { return buckets[a].ub > buckets[b].ub; });
208 }
209 }
210
211 for (auto &node : nodes) {
212 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
213 node.fw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
214 } else {
215 node.bw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
216 }
217 }
218
219 auto process_bucket_arcs = [&](int bucket, int scc_index) {
220 int from_node_id = buckets[bucket].node_id;
221 VRPNode &node = nodes[from_node_id];
222 const auto &node_arcs = (D == Direction::Forward) ? node.fw_arcs : node.bw_arcs;
223 std::vector<Arc> &filtered_arcs =
224 (D == Direction::Forward) ? node.fw_arcs_scc[scc_index] : node.bw_arcs_scc[scc_index];
225
226 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
227 for (const auto &arc : bucket_arcs) {
228 int to_node_id = buckets[arc.to_bucket].node_id;
229 auto it = std::find_if(node_arcs.begin(), node_arcs.end(),
230 [&to_node_id](const Arc &a) { return a.to == to_node_id; });
231 if (it != node_arcs.end()) { filtered_arcs.push_back(*it); }
232 }
233 };
234
235 int scc_ctr = 0;
236 for (const auto &scc : sccs) {
237 for (int bucket : scc) { process_bucket_arcs(bucket, scc_ctr); }
238 ++scc_ctr;
239 }
240
241 auto sortAndDedup = [](std::vector<Arc> &arcs) {
242 pdqsort(arcs.begin(), arcs.end(),
243 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) { return std::tie(a.from, a.to) < std::tie(b.from, b.to); });
244 auto last = std::unique(arcs.begin(), arcs.end(),
245 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) { return a.from == b.from && a.to == b.to; });
246 arcs.erase(last, arcs.end());
247 };
248
249 for (auto &node : nodes) {
250 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
251 for (auto &arcs : node.fw_arcs_scc) { sortAndDedup(arcs); }
252 } else {
253 for (auto &arcs : node.bw_arcs_scc) { sortAndDedup(arcs); }
254 }
255 }
256
257 std::vector<int> bucket_scc_rank(buckets.size(), -1);
258 for (size_t rank = 0; rank < ordered_sccs.size(); ++rank) {
259 for (int bucket : ordered_sccs[rank]) { bucket_scc_rank[bucket] = static_cast<int>(rank); }
260 }
261
262 UnionFind uf(ordered_sccs);
263 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
264 fw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
265 fw_topological_order = topological_order;
266 fw_sccs = sccs;
267 fw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
268 fw_bucket_scc_rank = std::move(bucket_scc_rank);
269 fw_union_find = uf;
270 } else {
271 bw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
272 bw_topological_order = topological_order;
273 bw_sccs = sccs;
274 bw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
275 bw_bucket_scc_rank = std::move(bucket_scc_rank);
276 bw_union_find = uf;
277 }
278}

◆ set_adjacency_list()

template<Symmetry SYM>
void BucketGraph::set_adjacency_list ( )

Definition at line 687 of file BucketUtils.h.

687 {
688 // === Step 1: Clear Existing Arcs from Each Node ===
689 for (auto &node : nodes) { node.clear_arcs(); }
690
691 // === Step 2: MST-Based Clustering ===
692 MST mst_solver(nodes, [this](int from, int to) { return getcij(from, to); });
693 auto mst = mst_solver.compute_mst();
694
695 // Collect and sort MST edge weights.
696 std::vector<double> edge_weights;
697 edge_weights.reserve(mst.size());
698 for (const auto &[weight, from, to] : mst) { edge_weights.push_back(weight); }
699 pdqsort(edge_weights.begin(), edge_weights.end());
700
701 // Compute theta as the 90th percentile (scaled down by 100).
702 size_t p90_index = static_cast<size_t>(0.9 * edge_weights.size());
703 double theta = (edge_weights[p90_index]) / 100.0;
704 print_info("Computed theta: {}\n", theta);
705
706 // Cluster nodes and build mapping: job_id -> cluster_id.
707 auto clusters = mst_solver.cluster(theta);
708 print_info("Number of clusters: {}\n", clusters.size());
709 std::vector<int> job_to_cluster(nodes.size(), -1);
710 for (int cluster_id = 0; cluster_id < clusters.size(); ++cluster_id) {
711 for (int job : clusters[cluster_id]) { job_to_cluster[job] = cluster_id; }
712 }
713
714 // === Step 3: Precompute Resource Information ===
715 // Find the index for the "time" resource.
716 auto time_it = std::find(options.resources.begin(), options.resources.end(), "time");
717 const int time_resource_idx =
718 (time_it != options.resources.end()) ? std::distance(options.resources.begin(), time_it) : -1;
719
720 // === Step 4: Process Arcs for Each Node ===
721
722 // Helper lambda to compute resource increments.
723 // Depending on symmetry, consumption is either taken as-is or averaged.
724 auto compute_resource_increments = [&](const VRPNode &from_node, const VRPNode &to_node, double travel_cost,
725 std::vector<double> &inc) {
726 const int nres = options.resources.size();
727 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
728 for (int r = 0; r < nres; ++r) {
729 inc[r] = (r == time_resource_idx) ? travel_cost + from_node.duration : from_node.consumption[r];
730 }
731 } else {
732 for (int r = 0; r < nres; ++r) {
733 inc[r] = (r == time_resource_idx) ? (from_node.duration / 2.0 + travel_cost + to_node.duration / 2.0)
734 : ((from_node.consumption[r] + to_node.consumption[r]) / 2.0);
735 }
736 }
737 };
738
739 // Lambda to add forward and reverse arcs from a given node.
740 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &curr_node) {
741 // Containers to accumulate arcs before insertion.
742 using ArcTuple = std::tuple<double, int, std::vector<double>, double>;
743 std::vector<ArcTuple> forward_arcs;
744 std::vector<ArcTuple> reverse_arcs;
745 forward_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
746 reverse_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
747
748 // Iterate over candidate next nodes.
749 for (const auto &next_node : nodes) {
750 // Skip if the candidate is a depot, the same node, or a pstep
751 // depot.
752 if (next_node.id == options.depot || curr_node.id == next_node.id ||
753 (options.pstep && (next_node.id == options.pstep_depot || next_node.id == options.pstep_end_depot))) {
754 continue;
755 }
756
757 double travel_cost = getcij(curr_node.id, next_node.id);
758 double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost;
759
760 // Skip self-loops (or if bucket indexes match; here using node
761 // id as bucket id).
762 if (curr_node.id == next_node.id) continue;
763
764 // Compute resource increments.
765 std::vector<double> res_inc(options.resources.size());
766 compute_resource_increments(curr_node, next_node, travel_cost, res_inc);
767
768 // Check feasibility: ensure resource consumption does not
769 // exceed next node's upper bound.
770 bool feasible = true;
771 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
772 if (numericutils::gt(curr_node.lb[r] + res_inc[r], next_node.ub[r])) {
773 feasible = false;
774 break;
775 }
776 }
777 if (!feasible) continue;
778
779 // Compute priorities based on cluster membership.
780 bool same_cluster = (job_to_cluster[curr_node.id] == job_to_cluster[next_node.id]);
781 double forward_priority = (same_cluster ? 5.0 : 1.0) + 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time;
782 double reverse_priority = (same_cluster ? 1.0 : 5.0) + 1.E-5 * curr_node.start_time;
783
784 // Collect arcs for forward and reverse directions.
785 forward_arcs.emplace_back(forward_priority, next_node.id, res_inc, cost_inc);
786 reverse_arcs.emplace_back(reverse_priority, next_node.id, res_inc, cost_inc);
787 }
788
789 // Insert the arcs into the corresponding nodes.
790 for (const auto &[priority, to_node, res_inc, cost_inc] : forward_arcs) {
791 nodes[curr_node.id].template add_arc<Direction::Forward>(curr_node.id, to_node, res_inc, cost_inc,
792 priority);
793 }
794 for (const auto &[priority, from_node, res_inc, cost_inc] : reverse_arcs) {
795 nodes[from_node].template add_arc<Direction::Backward>(from_node, curr_node.id, res_inc, cost_inc,
796 priority);
797 }
798 };
799
800 // Process each node, skipping the end depot.
801 for (const auto &node : nodes) {
802 if (node.id != options.end_depot) { add_arcs_for_node(node); }
803 }
804}

505

508 // subtracting next node's cost

520

522 if (from_bucket == to_bucket) continue; // Skip arcs that loop back to the same bucket
523
524 bool feasible = true; // Check feasibility based on resource constraints

526 if (node.lb[r] + res_inc[r] > next_node.ub[r]) {
527 feasible = false;
528 break;
529 }
530 }
531 if (!feasible) continue; // Skip infeasible arcs
532

534 double priority_value;

537 // Higher priority if both nodes are in the same cluster

539 // same-cluster priority
540 reverse_priority_value = 1.0 + 1.E-5 * node.start_time; // Adjust weight for
541 // same-cluster priority
542 } else {
543 // Lower priority for cross-cluster arcs
544 priority_value = 1.0 + 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time; // Higher base value for
545 // cross-cluster arcs
546 reverse_priority_value = 5.0 + 1.E-5 * node.start_time; // Higher base value for
547 // cross-cluster arcs
548 }
549

551 cost_inc); // Store the forward arc
552 best_arcs_rev.emplace_back(reverse_priority_value, next_node.id, res_inc,
553 cost_inc); // Store the reverse arc
554 }
555
556 // Add forward arcs from the current node to its neighbors
557 for (const auto &arc : best_arcs) {
558 auto [priority_value, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] = arc;
559 nodes[node.id].template add_arc<Direction::Forward>(node.id, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc,
560 priority_value); // Add forward arc
561 }
562
563 // Add reverse arcs from neighboring nodes to the current node

566 nodes[to_bucket].template add_arc<Direction::Backward>(to_bucket, node.id, res_inc_local, cost_inc,
567 priority_value); // Add reverse arc
568 }
569 };

572 for (const auto &VRPNode : nodes) {
573 if (VRPNode.id == options.end_depot) continue; // Skip the last node (depot)
574
575 std::vector<double> res_inc(intervals.size()); // Resource increment vector
576 add_arcs_for_node(VRPNode, VRPNode.id,
577 res_inc); // Add arcs for the current node
578 }

920 // std::vector<bool>(fw_buckets.size(), false));
921 // bw_fixed_buckets.assign(fw_buckets.size(),
922 // std::vector<bool>(fw_buckets.size(), false));
923
924 size_t fw_bitmap_size = (fw_buckets.size() * fw_buckets.size() + 63) / 64; // Round up division by 64
925 size_t bw_bitmap_size = (bw_buckets.size() * bw_buckets.size() + 63) / 64; // Round up division by 64
926 fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap.assign(fw_bitmap_size, 0);
927 bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap.assign(bw_bitmap_size, 0);
928
929 fw_arc_scores.resize(nodes.size());
930 bw_arc_scores.resize(nodes.size());
931
932 // define initial relationships
933 nodes.resize(options.size);
934 if (!options.manual_arcs) {
935 if (options.symmetric) {
936 set_adjacency_list<Symmetry::Symmetric>();
937 } else {
938 set_adjacency_list<Symmetry::Asymmetric>();
939 }
940 } else {
941 set_adjacency_list_manual();
942 }
943 generate_arcs();
944 for (auto &VRPNode : nodes) { VRPNode.assignSCCIds(nodes); }
945
946#ifdef SCHRODINGER
947 sPool.distance_matrix = distance_matrix;
948 sPool.setNodes(&nodes);
949 // sPool.setCutStorage(cut_storage);
950#endif
951
952 // Initialize the split
953 for (int i = 0; i < options.main_resources.size(); ++i) { q_star.push_back((R_max[i] - R_min[i] + 1) / 2); }
954}

1528 {
1529 int update_ctr = 0;
1530 ankerl::unordered_dense::set<int> updated_buckets;
1531
1532 // Iterate over all buckets and check if either forward or backward
1533 // bucket indicates a split is needed.
1534 for (size_t b = 0; b < fw_buckets.size(); ++b) {
1535 if (fw_buckets[b].shall_split || bw_buckets[b].shall_split) {
1536 const int inner_id = fw_buckets[b].node_id;
1537 // Skip depot buckets.
1538 if (inner_id == options.depot || inner_id == options.end_depot) { continue; }
1539 // If this node was already updated, skip it.
1540 if (updated_buckets.contains(inner_id)) { continue; }
1541 for (auto r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1542 // If the bucket split value is already at maximum (100),
1543 // skip.(127)
1544 //
1545 if (fw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] == 100) { continue; }
1546 // Update the bucket splits by doubling the current value,
1547 // capped at 100.
1548 const int update_target = std::min(fw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] * 2, 100);
1549 fw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] = update_target;
1550 bw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] = update_target;
1551 updated_buckets.insert(inner_id);
1552 }
1553 update_ctr++;
1554 }
1555 }
1556
1557 // If we have reached a significant number of updates and we haven't
1558 // accumulated too many cuts, then update bucket splits.
1559 const int CHANGE_THRESHOLD = nodes.size() / 6;
1560 if (update_ctr >= CHANGE_THRESHOLD && cut_storage->size() < 40) {
1561 print_info("Updating bucket splits with {} changes\n", update_ctr);
1562
1563 // Redefine buckets in parallel for both forward and backward
1564 // directions.
1565 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
1566 work, bi_sched,
1567 SECTION {
1568 // Forward bucket redefinition.
1569 define_buckets<Direction::Forward>();
1570 },
1571 SECTION {
1572 // Backward bucket redefinition.
1573 define_buckets<Direction::Backward>();
1574 });
1575
1576 // Regenerate arcs after updating bucket splits.
1577 generate_arcs();
1578
1579 // Process jump bucket arcs in parallel.
1580 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
1581 workB, bi_sched, SECTION { ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Forward>(); },
1582 SECTION { ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Backward>(); });
1583
1584 return true;
1585 }
1586 return false;
1587 }

649 {
650 if (!enumeration_policy.enabled || depth != 0) return false;

656

665 if (arc_count / denom > static_cast<size_t>(enumeration_policy.max_bucket_arcs_per_node)) return false;

667 return true;

◆ solvePSTEP()

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solvePSTEP ( PSTEPDuals & inner_pstep_duals)

Solves the PSTEP problem and returns a vector of labels representing paths.

This function performs the following steps:

  1. Resets the pool.
  2. Initializes the mono algorithm.
  3. Runs a labeling algorithm in the forward direction.
  4. Iterates through the forward buckets and computes new labels.
  5. Filters and collects labels that meet the criteria.
Returns
std::vector<Label*> A vector of pointers to Label objects representing the paths.

Definition at line 31 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

31 {
32
33 const int CHUNK_SIZE = 10;
34
35 std::vector<Label *> all_paths;
36 std::vector<double> all_costs;
37 std::vector<int> all_firsts, all_lasts;
38
39 // Mutex for shared resources
40 std::mutex cuts_mutex;
41
42 const int JOBS = nodes.size();
43 exec::static_thread_pool pool(std::thread::hardware_concurrency());
44 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
45
46 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> tasks;
47 for (int i = 0; i < JOBS - 1; ++i) {
48 for (int j = 1; j < JOBS; ++j) {
49 if (i == j) continue;
50 tasks.emplace_back(i, j);
51 }
52 }
53 auto bulk_sender =
54 stdexec::bulk(stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + CHUNK_SIZE - 1) / CHUNK_SIZE, // Calculate total chunks
55 [&](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
56 // Calculate indices i, j from chunk_idx
57 size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * CHUNK_SIZE;
58 size_t end_idx = std::min(start_idx + CHUNK_SIZE, tasks.size());
59 for (size_t task_idx = start_idx; task_idx < end_idx; ++task_idx) {
60 auto [i, j] = tasks[task_idx];
61 BucketOptions sub_options;
62 auto sub_bg = BucketGraph(nodes, R_max[0], 20);
63 sub_bg.set_distance_matrix(distance_matrix);
64 sub_options.depot = i;
65 sub_options.end_depot = j;
66 // print i and j
67 sub_options.pstep = true; // print depot and end_depot
68 sub_options.max_path_size = options.max_path_size;
69 sub_options.min_path_size = options.min_path_size;
70 sub_bg.setOptions(sub_options);
71 sub_bg.setPSTEPduals(inner_pstep_duals);
72 sub_bg.setup();
73 sub_bg.reset_pool();
74 sub_bg.mono_initialization();
75 // Solve and get the paths
76 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(sub_bg.fw_buckets.size());
77 forward_cbar = sub_bg.labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, Stage::Four, Full::PSTEP>();
78 std::vector<Label *> paths;
79 // print paths.size
80 for (auto bucket : std::ranges::iota_view(0, sub_bg.fw_buckets_size)) {
81 auto bucket_labels = sub_bg.fw_buckets[bucket].get_labels();
82 // print bucket_labels size
83 for (auto label : bucket_labels) {
84 if (!label) continue;
85 auto new_label = compute_mono_label(label);
86 if ((new_label->nodes_covered.size() > 1) &&
87 (new_label->nodes_covered.back() == sub_options.end_depot) &&
88 (new_label->nodes_covered.size() >= options.min_path_size &&
89 new_label->nodes_covered.size() <= options.max_path_size)) {
90 paths.push_back(new_label);
91 }
92 }
93 }
94
95 // Check if no paths are found
96 if (paths.empty()) return;
97
98 // Lock to protect shared resources
99 {
100 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(cuts_mutex);
101 // Process the found paths
102 for (const auto &path : paths) { all_paths.push_back(path); }
103 }
104 // }
105 }
106 });
107
108 // Submit work to the thread pool and wait for completion
109 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
110 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
111
112 return all_paths;
113}

363 {
364 int JOBS = nodes.size();
365
366 std::vector<std::vector<uint16_t>> all_paths;
