369 std::vector<int> all_lasts;
370
371 auto duals = solveTSP(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, true);
372 auto max_pstep_iterations = 10;
373 for (auto z = 0; z < max_pstep_iterations; ++z) {
374 fmt::print("--------------------------------------\n");
375 fmt::print("Iteration {}\n", z);
376 auto three_two_duals = std::get<0>(duals);
377 auto three_three_duals = std::get<1>(duals);
378 auto three_five_duals = std::get<2>(duals);
379 PSTEPDuals inner_pstep_duals;
380
381 // Convert three_two to vector of pairs
382 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_two_tuples;
383 three_two_tuples.reserve(three_two_duals.size());
384 std::transform(three_two_duals.begin(), three_two_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_two_tuples),
385 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
386 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeTwoDualValues(std::move(three_two_tuples));
387
388 // Convert three_three to vector of pairs
389 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_three_tuples;
390 three_three_tuples.reserve(three_three_duals.size());
391 std::transform(three_three_duals.begin(), three_three_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_three_tuples),
392 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
393 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeThreeDualValues(std::move(three_three_tuples));
394
395 // Convert three_five to vector of pairs of pairs
396 std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, double>> arc_duals_tuples;
397 size_t total_size = 0;
398 for (const auto &row : three_five_duals) total_size += row.size();
399 arc_duals_tuples.reserve(total_size);
400
401 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
402 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
403 if (i != j) { // Skip diagonal or add specific condition if needed
404 arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]);
405 }
406 }
407 }
408 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
409
410 auto sub_paths = solvePSTEP(inner_pstep_duals);
411 // print sub_paths.size
412 auto paths_added = 0;
413 for (auto path : sub_paths) {
414 if (path->cost >= 0) { continue; }
415 paths_added++;
416 all_paths.push_back(path->getRoute());
417 all_costs.push_back(path->real_cost);
418 all_firsts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.front());
419 all_lasts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.back());
420 }
421 if (paths_added == 0) { break; }
422
423 duals = solveTSP(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix);
424 }
425 return {};
426}

auto BucketGraph::solveTSP ( std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > & paths,
std::vector< double > & path_costs,

std::vector< std::vector< double > > & cost_matrix,

Definition at line 235 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

237 {
238 auto n = cost_matrix.size();
239 MIPProblem model = MIPProblem("tsp", 0, 0);
240 if (first_time) { generateInitialPaths(n - 1, paths, path_costs, firsts, lasts, cost_matrix); }
241 // Define variables
242 int R = paths.size(); // Number of p-steps
243 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> x(R); // Binary variables for each p-step
244 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> u(n); // Continuous variables for each node
245
246 // Create binary variables x_r for each p-step r
247 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
248 // fmt::print("path_costs[{}] = {}\n", i, path_costs[i]);
249 std::string varname = "x_" + std::to_string(i);
250 double custo = path_costs[i];
251 x[i] = model.add_variable(varname, VarType::Continuous, 0.0, 1.0, custo);
252 }
253
254 // Create continuous variables u_i for each node i
255 for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
256 auto name = "u_" + std::to_string(i);
257 u[i] = model.add_variable(name, VarType::Continuous, 0.0, n - 1, 0.0);
258 }
259 model.setObjectiveSense(ObjectiveType::Minimize);
260
261 // Constraints (3.3) and (3.4):
262 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_two(n);
263 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_three(n);
264 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
265 LinearExpression in_constraint;
266 LinearExpression out_constraint;
267 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
268 if (firsts[r] == i) { in_constraint += x[r]; }
269 if (lasts[r] == i) { in_constraint -= x[r]; }
270 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end() && i != lasts[r]) {
271 out_constraint += x[r];
272 }
273 }
274 three_two[i] = model.add_constraint(in_constraint == 0, "33_" + std::to_string(i));
275 three_three[i] = model.add_constraint(out_constraint == 1, "34_" + std::to_string(i));
276 }
277
278 // Constraints (3.5): Avoid sub-tours using MTZ constraints
279 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_five_constraints;
280 std::vector<std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>> three_five_constraints_matrix(n, std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>(n));
281 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
282 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
283 if (i != j) {
284 LinearExpression r_sum;
285 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
286 for (int k = 0; k < paths[r].size() - 1; ++k) {
287 if (paths[r][k] == i && paths[r][k + 1] == j) { r_sum += x[r]; }
288 }
289 }
290 auto ctr = u[i] - u[j] + (n - 1) * r_sum <= (n - 2);
291 three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j] =
292 model.add_constraint(ctr, "mtz_" + std::to_string(i) + "_" + std::to_string(j));
293 }
294 }
295 }
296
297 for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
298 model.add_constraint(u[i] >= 0, "u_lb_" + std::to_string(i));
299 model.add_constraint(u[i] <= n - 1, "u_ub_" + std::to_string(i));
300 }
301
302#ifdef IPM
303 IPSolver *ipSolver = new IPSolver();
304 auto matrix = model.extractModelDataSparse();
305 ipSolver->run_optimization(matrix, 1e-2);
306
307 auto duals = ipSolver->getDuals();
308#else
309 auto duals = std::vector<double>(N_SIZE, 0.0);
310#endif
311 std::vector<double> three_two_duals(n, 0.0);
312 std::vector<double> three_three_duals(n, 0.0);
313 std::vector<std::vector<double>> three_five_duals(n, std::vector<double>(n + 1, 0.0));
314 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
315 three_two_duals[i] = options.three_two_sign * duals[three_two[i]->index()];
316 three_three_duals[i] = options.three_three_sign * duals[three_three[i]->index()];
317 }
318
319 // Extract duals for three_five constraints
320 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
321 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
322 if (i != j) {
323 three_five_duals[i][j] =
324 options.three_five_sign * (n - 1) * duals[three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j]->index()];
325 }
326 }
327 }
328#ifdef IPM
329 auto lp_obj = ipSolver->getObjective();
330#else
331 auto lp_obj = 0.0;
332#endif
333 fmt::print("LP Objective: {}\n", lp_obj);
334 GRBEnv &env = GurobiEnvSingleton::getInstance();
335 GRBModel *modelGRB = model.toGurobiModel(env).release(); // Release ownership from unique_ptr
336 // set model verbose
337 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_OutputFlag, 0);
338 // set model type as 2
339 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_Method, 2);
340
341 // convert x vars to int and solve again
342 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
343 modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).set(GRB_CharAttr_VType, GRB_BINARY);
344 }
345 modelGRB->update();
346 modelGRB->optimize();
347
348 if (modelGRB->get(GRB_IntAttr_Status) == GRB_OPTIMAL) {
349 fmt::print("Integer solution found with value: {}\n", modelGRB->get(GRB_DoubleAttr_ObjVal));
350 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
351 if (modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).get(GRB_DoubleAttr_X) > 0.5) {
352 fmt::print("x_{} = {}\n", i, modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).get(GRB_DoubleAttr_X));
353 // print path nodes
354 for (auto node : paths[i]) { fmt::print("{} ", node); }
355 fmt::print("\n");
356 }
357 }
358 }
359
360 return std::make_tuple(three_two_duals, three_three_duals, three_five_duals);
361}

◆ solveTSPTW()

auto BucketGraph::solveTSPTW ( std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > & paths,
std::vector< double > & path_costs,
std::vector< int > & firsts,
std::vector< int > & lasts,
std::vector< std::vector< double > > & cost_matrix,
std::vector< double > & service_times,
std::vector< double > & time_windows_start,
std::vector< double > & time_windows_end,
bool first_time = false )

Definition at line 428 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

432 {
433 auto n = cost_matrix.size();
434 MIPProblem model = MIPProblem("tsptw", 0, 0);
435
436 if (first_time) {
437 generateInitialPathsTSPTW(n - 1, paths, path_costs, firsts, lasts, cost_matrix, time_windows_start,
438 time_windows_end, service_times);
439 }
440
441 // Variables
442 int R = paths.size();
443 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> x(R); // Binary variables for p-steps
444 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> omega(n); // Continuous variables for accumulated time
445
446 // Create binary variables x_r for each p-step r
447 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
448 x[i] = model.add_variable("x_" + std::to_string(i), VarType::Binary, 0.0, 1.0, path_costs[i]);
449 }
450
451 // Create continuous variables ω_i for time accumulation
452 for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
453 omega[i] = model.add_variable("omega_" + std::to_string(i), VarType::Continuous, 0.0, 1e8, 0.0);
454 }
455 model.setObjectiveSense(ObjectiveType::Minimize);
456
457 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_two(n);
458 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_three(n);
459 // Constraints (4.11): Flow balance for p-steps
460 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
461 LinearExpression flow_balance;
462 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
463 if (firsts[r] == i) flow_balance += x[r];
464 if (lasts[r] == i) flow_balance -= x[r];
465 }
466 three_two[i] = model.add_constraint(flow_balance == 0, "flow_" + std::to_string(i));
467 }
468
469 // Constraints (4.12): Visit each node at most once
470 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
471 LinearExpression visit_once;
472 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
473 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end()) { visit_once += x[r]; }
474 }
475 three_three[i] = model.add_constraint(visit_once <= 1, "visit_" + std::to_string(i));
476 }
477
478 // Constraint (4.13): Visit source exactly once
479 LinearExpression source_visit;
480 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
481 if (firsts[r] == 0) { source_visit += x[r]; }
482 }
483 model.add_constraint(source_visit == 1, "source");
484
485 // Constraints (4.14): Time accumulation with big-M
486 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_five_constraints;
487 std::vector<std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>> three_five_constraints_matrix(n, std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>(n));
488 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
489 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
490 if (i != j) {
491 double M_ij = 1e8;
492 LinearExpression time_acc;
493 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
494 for (size_t k = 0; k < paths[r].size() - 1; ++k) {
495 if (paths[r][k] == i && paths[r][k + 1] == j) { time_acc += x[r]; }
496 }
497 }
498 three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j] = model.add_constraint(
499 omega[j] - omega[i] - (cost_matrix[i][j] + service_times[i]) * time_acc - M_ij * time_acc >= -M_ij,
500 "time_" + std::to_string(i) + "_" + std::to_string(j));
501 }
502 }
503 }
504
505 // define omega[0] = 0
506
507 // Constraints (4.15): Time window constraints
508 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
509 LinearExpression node_used;
510 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
511 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end()) { node_used += x[r]; }
512 }
513 model.add_constraint(omega[i] - time_windows_start[i] * node_used >= 0, "tw_lb_" + std::to_string(i));
514 model.add_constraint(omega[i] - time_windows_end[i] * node_used <= 0, "tw_ub_" + std::to_string(i));
515 }
516
517// Solve the model
518#ifdef IPM
519 IPSolver *ipSolver = new IPSolver();
520 auto matrix = model.extractModelDataSparse();
521 ipSolver->run_optimization(matrix, 1e-2);
522
523 // Extract solution and duals
524 auto duals = ipSolver->getDuals();
525#else
526 auto duals = std::vector<double>(N_SIZE, 0.0);
527#endif
528 std::vector<double> three_two_duals(n, 0.0);
529 std::vector<double> three_three_duals(n, 0.0);
530 std::vector<std::vector<double>> three_five_duals(n, std::vector<double>(n + 1, 0.0));
531 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
532 three_two_duals[i] = options.three_two_sign * duals[three_two[i]->index()];
533 three_three_duals[i] = options.three_three_sign * duals[three_three[i]->index()];
534 fmt::print("Duals for node {}: 3.2 = {}, 3.3 = {}\n", i, three_two_duals[i], three_three_duals[i]);
535 }
536
537 // Extract duals for three_five constraints
538 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
539 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
540 if (i != j) {
541 three_five_duals[i][j] =
542 options.three_five_sign * (n - 1) * duals[three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j]->index()];
543 }
544 }
545 }
546
547 // Solution output and return
548 // fmt::print("LP Objective: {}\n", ipSolver->getObjective());
549
550 GRBEnv &env = GurobiEnvSingleton::getInstance();
551
552 GRBModel *modelGRB = model.toGurobiModel(env).release();
553 // set model verbose
554 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_OutputFlag, 1);
555 // set model type as 2
556 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_Method, 0);
557
558 modelGRB->update();
559 modelGRB->optimize();
560
561 return std::make_tuple(three_two_duals, three_three_duals, three_five_duals);
562}

564 {
565 int JOBS = nodes.size();
566
567 // Initialize containers for storing paths, costs, firsts, and lasts
568 std::vector<std::vector<uint16_t>> all_paths;
569 std::vector<double> all_costs;
570 std::vector<int> all_firsts;
571 std::vector<int> all_lasts;
572
573 // extract service time from nodes
574 std::vector<double> service_times;
575 std::vector<double> time_windows_start(JOBS, 0.0);
576 std::vector<double> time_windows_end(JOBS, 10000.0);
577 for (int i = 0; i < JOBS; ++i) {
578 time_windows_start[i] = nodes[i].lb[0];
579 time_windows_end[i] = nodes[i].ub[0];
580 service_times.push_back(nodes[i].duration);
581 }
582 // Step 1: Solve the initial TSPTW using the previously defined method
583 auto duals = solveTSPTW(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, service_times,
584 time_windows_start, time_windows_end, true);
585
586 // Step 2: Set up iteration for generating new paths using dual values
587 int max_pstep_iterations = 10;
588 for (int z = 0; z < max_pstep_iterations; ++z) {
589 fmt::print("--------------------------------------\n");
590 fmt::print("Iteration {}\n", z);
591
592 // Extract dual values for constraints
593 auto three_two_duals = std::get<0>(duals);
594 auto three_three_duals = std::get<1>(duals);
595 auto three_five_duals = std::get<2>(duals);
596 // auto arrival_time_duals = std::get<3>(duals);
597
598 PSTEPDuals inner_pstep_duals;
599
600 // Convert dual values for constraint (3.32)
601 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_two_tuples;
602 three_two_tuples.reserve(three_two_duals.size());
603 std::transform(three_two_duals.begin(), three_two_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_two_tuples),
604 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
605 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeTwoDualValues(std::move(three_two_tuples));
606
607 // Convert dual values for constraint (3.33)
608 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_three_tuples;
609 three_three_tuples.reserve(three_three_duals.size());
610 std::transform(three_three_duals.begin(), three_three_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_three_tuples),
611 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
612 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeThreeDualValues(std::move(three_three_tuples));
613
614 // Convert dual values for MTZ constraints (3.34) and time windows (3.35)
615 std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, double>> arc_duals_tuples;
616 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
617 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
618 if (i != j) { arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]); }
619 }
620 }
621 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
622
623 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
624 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
625 if (i != j) { // Skip diagonal or add specific condition if needed
626 arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]);
627 }
628 }
629 }
630 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
631
632 // Step 3: Solve the subproblem to generate new p-steps
633 fmt::print("Solving PSTEP subproblem\n");
634 auto sub_paths = solvePSTEP(inner_pstep_duals);
635 fmt::print("Subproblem solved\n");
636
637 // Add the new paths to the existing list if valid
638 int paths_added = 0;
639 for (auto path : sub_paths) {
640 if (path->cost >= 0) { continue; }
641 paths_added++;
642 all_paths.push_back(path->getRoute());
643 all_costs.push_back(path->real_cost);
644 all_firsts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.front());
645 all_lasts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.back());
646 }
647
648 // If no new paths were added, stop the iteration
649 if (paths_added == 0) { break; }
650
651 // Step 4: Re-solve the TSPTW with the updated set of p-steps
652 duals = solveTSPTW(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, service_times,
653 time_windows_start, time_windows_end);
654 }
655
656 return {};
657}

867 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
868 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
869 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
870
871 // Determine the base interval and other relevant values for
872 // each resource
873 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
874 total_ranges[r] = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
875 base_intervals[r] = total_ranges[r] / intervals[r].interval;
876 }
877
878 int original_num_buckets = buckets_size;
879
880 // To track splits per node_id
881 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, int> splits_per_node;
882
883 // Loop until the second-to-last bucket to avoid
884 // out-of-bounds access
885 for (int i = 0; i < original_num_buckets - 1; ++i) {
886 const auto &bucket = buckets[i];
887
888 // Calculate mid-point for each dimension
889 std::vector<double> mid_point(bucket.lb.size());
890 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
891 for (int r = 0; r < bucket.lb.size(); ++r) { mid_point[r] = (bucket.lb[r] + base_intervals[r]) / 2.0; }
892 } else {
893 for (int r = 0; r < bucket.lb.size(); ++r) { mid_point[r] = (bucket.ub[r] - base_intervals[r]) / 2.0; }
894 }
895
896 // Create two new buckets by splitting at the mid-point
897 Bucket bucket1, bucket2;
898 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
899 bucket1 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, bucket.ub);
900 bucket2 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, mid_point, bucket.ub);
901 } else {
902 bucket1 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, bucket.ub);
903 bucket2 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, mid_point);
904 }
905
906 // Manually shift elements to the right to make space
907 // for the new bucket
908 for (int j = original_num_buckets; j > i + 1; --j) { buckets[j] = buckets[j - 1]; }
909
910 // Insert the new bucket at position i+1
911 buckets[i + 1] = bucket2;
912
913 // Replace the current bucket with the first half
914 // (bucket1)
915 buckets[i] = bucket1;
916
917 // Track the number of splits for this node_id
918 splits_per_node[bucket.node_id]++;
919
920 // Since we added a new bucket at i+1, increment i to
921 // skip over the new bucket
922 ++i;
923 ++original_num_buckets; // Adjust the count to account
924 // for the new bucket
925 }
926
927 // Update the global bucket size
928 buckets_size = original_num_buckets;
929
930 // Now update the num_buckets_index for each node_id based
931 // on the final positions
932 int current_index = 0;
933 for (int i = 0; i < original_num_buckets; ++i) {
934 const auto &bucket = buckets[i];
935 if (num_buckets_index[bucket.node_id] == -1) { num_buckets_index[bucket.node_id] = current_index; }
936 current_index++;
937 }
938 }

95 {
96 if (result.skip_stage_transition) return;
97
98 switch (active_stage) {
99 case Stage::One:
100 if (result.best_reduced_cost >= -1) { activate_bucket_pricing_stage(Stage::Two); }
101 break;
102 case Stage::Two:
103 if (result.best_reduced_cost >= -10) { activate_bucket_pricing_stage(Stage::Three); }
104 break;
105 case Stage::Three:
106 if (result.best_reduced_cost >= -0.5) {
107 activate_bucket_pricing_stage(Stage::Four);
108 transition = true;
109 }
110 break;
111 case Stage::Four: {
112#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
113 if (transition) { break; }
114#endif
115 if (status != Status::Rollback && considerRegenerate()) {
116 status = Status::Rollback;
117 break;
118 }
119 threshold = stats.computeThreshold(iter, result.best_reduced_cost);
120 constexpr double kExactPricingTol = 1e-6;
121 if (result.best_reduced_cost >= -kExactPricingTol) {
122 ss = true;
123#if !defined(SRC) && !defined(SRC3)
124 status = Status::Optimal;
125#else
126 status = Status::Separation;
127#endif
128 } else {
129 ss = false;
130 status = Status::NotOptimal;
131 }
132 break;
133 }
134 case Stage::Enumerate:
135 if (result.enumeration_failed) {
136 activate_bucket_pricing_stage(Stage::Four);
137 status = Status::NotOptimal;
138 } else {
139 status = Status::Optimal;
140 }
141 break;
142 default:
143 break;
144 }

◆ UpdateBucketsSet()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::UpdateBucketsSet ( double theta,
const Label * label,
ankerl::unordered_dense::set< int > & Bbidi,
int & current_bucket,
std::vector< uint64_t > & Bvisited )

Updates the set of buckets based on the given parameters.

This function inserts the current bucket into the visited set and then performs various checks and updates based on the provided theta, label, Bbidi, current_bucket, and Bvisited parameters.

30
31 // Cache label values to avoid repeated member lookups.
32 const int bucketLnode = label->node_id;

36
37 // Precompute a flag for direction (avoid repeated ternary operators).
38 constexpr bool forward = (D == Direction::Forward);
39
40 // Lambda to check if the visited bitmaps of L and L_bw overlap.
41 auto overlapsVisited = [n_bitmap, this](const Label *L, const Label *L_bw) -> bool {
42 // Make a local copy of the visited bitmap.
43 auto visited_bitmap = L->visited_bitmap;

45 // check if visited_bitmap overlaps at all

67
68 // Use a local DFS stack to traverse buckets.
69 static thread_local std::vector<int> bucket_stack;
70 bucket_stack.clear();
71 bucket_stack.reserve(32);
72 bucket_stack.push_back(current_bucket);
73
74 // Process buckets until the stack is empty.
75 while (!bucket_stack.empty()) {
76 const int curr_bucket = bucket_stack.back();
77 bucket_stack.pop_back();
78
79 // Skip if already visited.
80 const size_t segment = curr_bucket >> 6;
81 const size_t bit_pos = curr_bucket & 63;
82 if ((Bvisited[segment] & (1ULL << bit_pos)) != 0) { continue; }
83
84 // Mark the current bucket as visited.
85 Bvisited[segment] |= (1ULL << bit_pos);
86
87 // Get the opposite bucket’s label and node id.
88 const int bucketLprimenode = buckets_opposite[curr_bucket].node_id;
89 double cost = getcij(bucketLnode, bucketLprimenode);
90
91 // Optionally adjust cost with arc duals if RCC mode is enabled.
92 RCC_MODE_BLOCK(auto arc_dual = arc_duals.getDual(bucketLnode, bucketLprimenode); cost -= arc_dual;)
93
94 // Skip if the accumulated cost plus lower bound exceeds theta.
95 if (numericutils::gt(label_cost + cost + c_bar_opposite[curr_bucket], theta)) continue;
96
97 const auto &opposite_labels = buckets_opposite[curr_bucket].get_labels();
98 for (const auto &L_opposite : opposite_labels) {
99 if (L_opposite == nullptr || L_opposite->is_dominated) continue;
100
101 // Skip labels from the same node.
102 if (bucketLnode == L_opposite->node_id) continue;
103
104 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
105 if (overlapsVisited(label, L_opposite)) continue;
106 } else {
107 if (overlapsVisited(L_opposite, label)) continue;
108 }
109
110 // Determine the reference node for resource checks.
111 const VRPNode &ref_node = forward ? nodes[bucketLnode] : nodes[L_opposite->node_id];
112
113 bool violated = false;
114 const size_t num_resources = options.resources.size();
115 for (size_t r = 0; r < num_resources; ++r) {
116 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
117 const double time_constraint = forward
118 ? label->resources[TIME_INDEX] + cost + ref_node.duration
119 : L_opposite->resources[TIME_INDEX] + cost + ref_node.duration;
120 if (numericutils::gt(time_constraint,
121 forward ? L_opposite->resources[TIME_INDEX] : label->resources[TIME_INDEX])) {
122 violated = true;
123 break;
124 }
125 } else {
126 const double resource_constraint = forward ? label->resources[r] + ref_node.consumption[r]
127 : L_opposite->resources[r] + ref_node.consumption[r];
128 if (numericutils::gt(resource_constraint,
129 forward ? L_opposite->resources[r] : label->resources[r])) {
130 violated = true;
131 break;
132 }
133 }
134 }
135
136 if (violated) continue;
137
138 // Record only buckets that have at least one theta-compatible
139 // label pair.
140 if (numericutils::lt(label_cost + cost + L_opposite->cost, theta)) {
141 Bbidi.emplace(curr_bucket);
142 break;
143 }
144 }
145
146 // Add unvisited neighbor buckets.
147 for (int b_prime : Phi_opposite[curr_bucket]) {
148 const size_t seg_prime = b_prime >> 6;
149 const size_t bit_prime = b_prime & 63;
150 if ((Bvisited[seg_prime] & (1ULL << bit_prime)) == 0) bucket_stack.push_back(b_prime);
151 }
152 }
153}

◆ updateSplit()

void BucketGraph::updateSplit ( )
inline

Definition at line 1053 of file BucketGraph.h.

1053 {
1054 // Base thresholds and parameters for adjustments.
1055 constexpr double BASE_IMBALANCE_THRESHOLD = 0.10; // Lower threshold —
1056 // work-weighted signal
1057 // is more sensitive
1058 // than raw counts.
1059 constexpr double MIN_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR = 0.002;
1060 constexpr double MAX_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR = 0.015;
1061 constexpr double EMA_ALPHA = 0.1; // Smoothing factor for EMA.
1062 constexpr int SPLIT_UPDATE_COOLDOWN = 5;
1063
1064 // Prefer a work-weighted signal: total labels scanned during dominance
1065 // walks captures both check count and per-bin depth, which is what the
1066 // labeler actually pays for. Fall back to label counts when the
1067 // profiler hasn't recorded data yet (first iteration / profiling off).
1068 // Sum across stages because Stage 1-3 may be active early and Stage 4
1069 // dominates later iterations.
1070 uint64_t fw_work = 0, bw_work = 0;
1071 for (size_t i = 0; i < PROFILE_STAGE_COUNT; ++i) {
1072 fw_work += profile_inner_bin_scan_labels_fw[i];
1073 bw_work += profile_inner_bin_scan_labels_bw[i];
1074 }
1075 const bool have_work_signal = (fw_work + bw_work) > 0;
1076 const double fw_labels = have_work_signal ? static_cast<double>(fw_work) : static_cast<double>(n_fw_labels);
1077 const double bw_labels = have_work_signal ? static_cast<double>(bw_work) : static_cast<double>(n_bw_labels);
1078 const double total_labels = fw_labels + bw_labels;
1079
1080 // Skip adjustment if too few labels exist.
1081 if (total_labels < 10) return;
1082
1083 // Compute imbalance ratios for forward and backward labels.
1084 const double fw_ratio = fw_labels / total_labels;
1085 const double bw_ratio = bw_labels / total_labels;
1086 const double imbalance_ratio = std::abs(fw_ratio - bw_ratio);
1087
1088 // Update exponential moving average (EMA) of the imbalance ratio.
1089 split_imbalance_ema = EMA_ALPHA * imbalance_ratio + (1.0 - EMA_ALPHA) * split_imbalance_ema;
1090
1091 // Keep a real dead-band even on large passes. Letting the threshold
1092 // decay to zero makes q_star chase noise and creates concat churn.
1093 const double dynamic_threshold = std::max(0.03, BASE_IMBALANCE_THRESHOLD * std::exp(-total_labels / 50000.0));
1094 if (iter - last_split_update_iter < SPLIT_UPDATE_COOLDOWN) return;
1095
1096 // Only adjust split if the imbalance exceeds the dynamic threshold.
1097 if (split_imbalance_ema > dynamic_threshold) {
1098 // Severity scales between 0 and 1.
1099 const double severity = (split_imbalance_ema - dynamic_threshold) / (1.0 - dynamic_threshold);
1100
1101 // Adaptive adjustment factor increases with severity.
1102 const double adjustment_factor =
1103 MIN_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR + (MAX_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR - MIN_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR) * severity;
1104
1105 // Compute resource range based on the main resource.
1106 const double resource_range = R_max[options.main_resources[0]] - R_min[options.main_resources[0]];
1107
1108 // Base adjustment scales with the resource range; no decay so
1109 // adjustments stay aggressive when imbalance persists late.
1110 const double base_adjustment = adjustment_factor * resource_range;
1111 last_split_update_iter = iter;
1112
1113 // Adjust each split position in q_star.
1114 for (size_t i = 0; i < q_star.size(); ++i) {
1115 // Compute relative position (0: min, 1: max) within the
1116 // resource range.
1117 const double relative_pos = (q_star[i] - R_min[options.main_resources[0]]) / resource_range;
1118
1119 // Position factor gives smaller adjustments near boundaries.
1120 const double position_factor = 4.0 * relative_pos * (1.0 - relative_pos);
1121
1122 // Determine direction: shift toward balancing label counts.
1123 const double direction = (bw_labels > fw_labels) ? 1.0 : -1.0;
1124
1125 // Final adjustment for this split.
1126 const double final_adjustment = base_adjustment * position_factor * direction;
1127
1128 // Apply the adjustment with boundary protection.
1129 q_star[i] = std::clamp(q_star[i] + final_adjustment,
1130 R_min[options.main_resources[0]] + resource_range * 0.1, // Lower bound.
1131 R_max[options.main_resources[0]] - resource_range * 0.1 // Upper bound.
1132 );
1133 }
1134
1135 // Log adjustments if the base adjustment is significant.
1136 if (std::abs(base_adjustment) > resource_range * 0.01) {
1137 print_info("Split adjustment: imbalance={:.3f}, adjustment={:.3f}\n", split_imbalance_ema,
1138 base_adjustment);
1139 }
1140 }
1141 }
